#!/bin/bash

# SprintSpark Server Management Script
# Manages deployment, testing, health checks, and operations

set -e

# Colors for output
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
BLUE='\033[0;34m'
PURPLE='\033[0;35m'
CYAN='\033[0;36m'
NC='\033[0m' # No Color

# Server connection
REMOTE_HOST="31.97.102.48"
REMOTE_USER="ubuntu"
REMOTE_SERVER="${REMOTE_USER}@${REMOTE_HOST}"

# Staging environment
STAGING_DEPLOY_PATH="/home/ubuntu/sprintspark-staging"
STAGING_DOMAIN="sprintspark.biswas.me"
STAGING_API_PORT=8082
STAGING_WEB_PORT=8086
STAGING_MCP_PORT=8084
STAGING_MCP_DOMAIN="mcp.sprintspark.biswas.me"

# Production environment
PROD_DEPLOY_PATH="/home/ubuntu/sprintspark"
PROD_DOMAIN="taskai.cc"
PROD_API_PORT=8081
PROD_WEB_PORT=8085
PROD_MCP_PORT=8083
PROD_MCP_DOMAIN="mcp.taskai.cc"

# Local development settings
LOCAL_WEB_PORT=8084
LOCAL_API_PORT=8083

# Resolve environment — sets REMOTE_DEPLOY_PATH, REMOTE_DOMAIN, REMOTE_API_PORT, REMOTE_WEB_PORT
resolve_env() {
    local env="${1:-staging}"
    case "$env" in
        prod|production)
            REMOTE_DEPLOY_PATH="$PROD_DEPLOY_PATH"
            REMOTE_DOMAIN="$PROD_DOMAIN"
            REMOTE_API_PORT="$PROD_API_PORT"
            REMOTE_WEB_PORT="$PROD_WEB_PORT"
            REMOTE_MCP_PORT="$PROD_MCP_PORT"
            REMOTE_MCP_DOMAIN="$PROD_MCP_DOMAIN"
            ENV_LABEL="PRODUCTION"
            ;;
        staging|*)
            REMOTE_DEPLOY_PATH="$STAGING_DEPLOY_PATH"
            REMOTE_DOMAIN="$STAGING_DOMAIN"
            REMOTE_API_PORT="$STAGING_API_PORT"
            REMOTE_WEB_PORT="$STAGING_WEB_PORT"
            REMOTE_MCP_PORT="$STAGING_MCP_PORT"
            REMOTE_MCP_DOMAIN="$STAGING_MCP_DOMAIN"
            ENV_LABEL="STAGING"
            ;;
    esac
}

# Function to print colored output
print_status() {
    echo -e "${GREEN}[INFO]${NC} $1"
}

print_warning() {
    echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARNING]${NC} $1"
}

print_error() {
    echo -e "${RED}[ERROR]${NC} $1"
}

print_success() {
    echo -e "${GREEN}[SUCCESS]${NC} $1"
}

print_header() {
    echo -e "${BLUE}=== $1 ===${NC}"
}

# Function to show help
show_help() {
    echo -e "${CYAN}SprintSpark Server Management Script${NC}"
    echo ""
    echo "Usage: $0 <command> [options]"
    echo ""
    echo -e "${YELLOW}Local Development Commands:${NC}"
    echo "  local dev            Start local dev mode with hot-reload (API + Web)"
    echo "  local:start          Start local Docker services (http://localhost:$LOCAL_WEB_PORT)"
    echo "  local:stop           Stop local Docker services"
    echo "  local:restart        Restart local Docker services"
    echo "  local:status         Show local service status"
    echo "  local:logs [service] Show local logs"
    echo "  local:clean          Stop and remove all local containers/volumes"
    echo ""
    echo -e "${YELLOW}Deployment Commands:${NC}"
    echo "  deploy [\"message\"]   Commit, push to main, auto-deploy to STAGING"
    echo "  promote [ref]        Promote to PRODUCTION (triggers GitHub Action)"
    echo ""
    echo -e "${YELLOW}Staging Commands (sprintspark.biswas.me):${NC}"
    echo "  staging status       Show staging service status"
    echo "  staging logs [svc]   Show staging logs"
    echo "  staging health       Check staging health"
    echo "  staging restart      Restart staging services"
    echo "  staging admin create <email>  Grant admin on staging"
    echo "  staging admin list            List admins on staging"
    echo "  staging admin revoke <email>  Revoke admin on staging"
    echo ""
    echo -e "${YELLOW}Production Commands (taskai.cc):${NC}"
    echo "  prod status          Show production service status"
    echo "  prod logs [svc]      Show production logs"
    echo "  prod health          Check production health"
    echo "  prod restart         Restart production services"
    echo "  prod admin create <email>    Grant admin on production"
    echo "  prod admin list              List admins on production"
    echo "  prod admin revoke <email>    Revoke admin on production"
    echo ""
    echo -e "${YELLOW}Test Commands:${NC}"
    echo "  test                 Run all tests (backend + frontend + coverage)"
    echo "  test-api             Run Go API tests"
    echo "  test-web             Run frontend tests"
    echo "  test-e2e             Run Playwright E2E tests"
    echo ""
    echo -e "${YELLOW}Admin Commands:${NC}"
    echo "  admin create <email> Create admin user (production)"
    echo "  admin list           List all admins (production)"
    echo "  admin revoke <email> Revoke admin privileges (production)"
    echo "  remote admin ...     Execute admin command on production"
    echo "  local admin ...      Execute admin command on local database"
    echo ""
    echo -e "${YELLOW}Database Commands:${NC}"
    echo "  db-migrate <src> <dst> [--yes]  Copy database between environments (DESTRUCTIVE)"
    echo "  db-query <query>                Execute SQL on production database"
    echo ""
    echo -e "${YELLOW}Utility Commands:${NC}"
    echo "  validate-openapi     Validate OpenAPI spec"
    echo ""
    echo -e "${YELLOW}Examples:${NC}"
    echo "  $0 deploy \"feat: add dark mode\"       # Deploy to staging"
    echo "  $0 promote                             # Promote main to production"
    echo "  $0 promote abc123                      # Promote specific commit"
    echo "  $0 staging health                      # Check staging health"
    echo "  $0 prod health                         # Check production health"
    echo "  $0 staging logs api                    # View staging API logs"
    echo "  $0 db-migrate staging prod             # Copy staging DB to production"
    echo "  $0 db-migrate prod staging             # Copy production DB to staging"
}

# Deploy to staging (commit, push, GitHub Actions handles staging deploy)
deploy_to_staging() {
    local commit_message="${1:-Deploy: automated deployment}"

    print_header "Deploying to Staging"

    # Check for uncommitted changes
    if [ -n "$(git status --porcelain)" ]; then
        print_status "Uncommitted changes detected, committing..."
        git add -A
        git commit -m "$commit_message

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>" || {
            print_error "Failed to commit changes"
            return 1
        }
    else
        print_status "No uncommitted changes found"
    fi

    # Push to remote
    print_status "Pushing to GitHub (triggers staging deploy)..."
    git push origin main || {
        print_error "Failed to push to GitHub"
        return 1
    }

    print_success "Code pushed to GitHub — staging deployment triggered"
    print_status "Monitor: https://github.com/anchoo2kewl/SprintSpark/actions"
    echo ""

    # Wait and verify staging
    print_status "Waiting for staging deployment (60 seconds)..."
    sleep 60

    resolve_env staging
    print_status "Verifying staging deployment..."
    local max_attempts=20
    local attempt=1

    while [ $attempt -le $max_attempts ]; do
        if curl -f -s "https://$STAGING_DOMAIN/api/health" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            print_success "Staging deployment successful!"
            echo ""
            echo -e "${CYAN}Staging URL:${NC} https://$STAGING_DOMAIN"
            echo -e "${CYAN}API Health:${NC} https://$STAGING_DOMAIN/api/health"
            echo ""
            print_status "To promote to production: $0 promote"
            return 0
        fi

        print_status "Waiting for staging to be ready (attempt $attempt/$max_attempts)..."
        sleep 5
        attempt=$((attempt + 1))
    done

    print_warning "Staging not responding yet — deployment may still be in progress"
    print_status "Check status: $0 staging health"
    print_status "Check actions: https://github.com/anchoo2kewl/SprintSpark/actions"
    return 1
}

# Promote to production (triggers GitHub Actions workflow)
promote_to_production() {
    local ref="${1:-main}"

    print_header "Promoting to Production"
    echo -e "${CYAN}Ref:${NC} $ref"
    echo ""

    # Check if gh CLI is available
    if ! command -v gh &> /dev/null; then
        print_error "GitHub CLI (gh) is not installed"
        print_status "Install: brew install gh"
        return 1
    fi

    # Trigger the production deployment workflow
    print_status "Triggering production deployment..."
    gh workflow run deploy-production.yml \
        -f ref="$ref" \
        -f confirm="deploy" || {
        print_error "Failed to trigger production deployment"
        return 1
    }

    print_success "Production deployment triggered!"
    echo ""
    echo -e "${CYAN}Monitor:${NC} https://github.com/anchoo2kewl/SprintSpark/actions"
    echo ""
    print_status "After deployment completes, verify: $0 prod health"
}

# Environment-specific operations
env_restart() {
    local env="$1"
    resolve_env "$env"
    print_header "Restarting $ENV_LABEL Services"

    ssh "$REMOTE_SERVER" "cd '$REMOTE_DEPLOY_PATH' && docker compose restart"

    print_success "$ENV_LABEL services restarted"
    sleep 3
    env_status "$env"
}

env_stop() {
    local env="$1"
    resolve_env "$env"
    print_header "Stopping $ENV_LABEL Services"

    ssh "$REMOTE_SERVER" "cd '$REMOTE_DEPLOY_PATH' && docker compose stop"

    print_success "$ENV_LABEL services stopped"
}

env_start() {
    local env="$1"
    resolve_env "$env"
    print_header "Starting $ENV_LABEL Services"

    ssh "$REMOTE_SERVER" "cd '$REMOTE_DEPLOY_PATH' && docker compose up -d"

    print_success "$ENV_LABEL services started"
    sleep 3
    env_status "$env"
}

env_status() {
    local env="$1"
    resolve_env "$env"
    print_header "$ENV_LABEL Service Status"

    ssh "$REMOTE_SERVER" "cd '$REMOTE_DEPLOY_PATH' && docker compose ps"
}

env_logs() {
    local env="$1"
    local service="${2:-}"
    resolve_env "$env"

    print_header "$ENV_LABEL Logs${service:+: $service}"

    if [ -z "$service" ]; then
        ssh "$REMOTE_SERVER" "cd '$REMOTE_DEPLOY_PATH' && docker compose logs --tail=50"
    else
        ssh "$REMOTE_SERVER" "cd '$REMOTE_DEPLOY_PATH' && docker compose logs --tail=50 $service"
    fi
}

env_health() {
    local env="$1"
    resolve_env "$env"
    print_header "$ENV_LABEL Health Check"

    # Check API
    print_status "Checking API health..."
    if curl -f -s "https://$REMOTE_DOMAIN/api/health" | jq . >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo -e "${GREEN}✓ API${NC} - Healthy (https://$REMOTE_DOMAIN/api/health)"
    else
        echo -e "${RED}✗ API${NC} - Not responding"
    fi

    # Check web
    print_status "Checking web UI..."
    if curl -f -s -I "https://$REMOTE_DOMAIN/" | head -1 | grep -q "200\|301\|302"; then
        echo -e "${GREEN}✓ Web UI${NC} - Healthy (https://$REMOTE_DOMAIN/)"
    else
        echo -e "${RED}✗ Web UI${NC} - Not responding"
    fi

    # Check MCP
    print_status "Checking MCP server..."
    if curl -f -s "https://$REMOTE_MCP_DOMAIN/health" | grep -q "ok"; then
        echo -e "${GREEN}✓ MCP${NC} - Healthy (https://$REMOTE_MCP_DOMAIN/health)"
    else
        echo -e "${YELLOW}⚠ MCP${NC} - Not responding (https://$REMOTE_MCP_DOMAIN/health)"
    fi

    # Check SonarQube (production only)
    if [ "$env" = "production" ]; then
        print_status "Checking SonarQube..."
        if curl -f -s "https://sonar.taskai.cc/api/system/status" | grep -q '"UP"'; then
            echo -e "${GREEN}✓ SonarQube${NC} - Healthy (https://sonar.taskai.cc)"
        else
            echo -e "${YELLOW}⚠ SonarQube${NC} - Not responding (https://sonar.taskai.cc)"
        fi
    fi

    # Check database via SSH
    print_status "Checking database..."
    if ssh "$REMOTE_SERVER" "cd '$REMOTE_DEPLOY_PATH' && docker compose exec -T api sh -c 'wget -q -O- http://localhost:8080/api/health'" 2>/dev/null | grep -q "ok"; then
        echo -e "${GREEN}✓ Database${NC} - Accessible from API"
    else
        echo -e "${YELLOW}⚠ Database${NC} - Unable to verify"
    fi
}

# Run API tests
test_api() {
    print_header "Running API Tests"

    cd api
    go test -v -cover ./... | tee /tmp/sprintspark-api-test.log
    local exit_code=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
    cd ..

    if [ $exit_code -eq 0 ]; then
        print_success "API tests passed"
    else
        print_error "API tests failed"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Run frontend tests
test_web() {
    print_header "Running Frontend Tests"

    cd web
    npm test 2>&1 | tee /tmp/sprintspark-web-test.log || {
        print_warning "Frontend tests not configured or failed"
        cd ..
        return 0
    }
    cd ..

    print_success "Frontend tests passed"
}

# Run E2E tests
test_e2e() {
    print_header "Running Playwright E2E Tests"

    cd web
    npx playwright test 2>&1 | tee /tmp/sprintspark-e2e-test.log
    local exit_code=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
    cd ..

    if [ $exit_code -eq 0 ]; then
        print_success "E2E tests passed"
    else
        print_error "E2E tests failed"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Run all tests and show summary
run_all_tests() {
    print_header "Running All Tests"

    local api_result=0
    local web_result=0
    local e2e_result=0

    # Run API tests
    test_api || api_result=1
    echo ""

    # Run web tests
    test_web || web_result=1
    echo ""

    # Run E2E tests
    test_e2e || e2e_result=1
    echo ""

    # Show coverage
    print_header "Test Coverage"

    if [ -f /tmp/sprintspark-api-test.log ]; then
        echo -e "${CYAN}API Coverage:${NC}"
        grep "coverage:" /tmp/sprintspark-api-test.log | tail -1 || echo "  No coverage data"
    fi

    # Summary
    print_header "Test Summary"

    if [ $api_result -eq 0 ]; then
        echo -e "${GREEN}✅ API Tests${NC} - Passed"
    else
        echo -e "${RED}❌ API Tests${NC} - Failed"
    fi

    if [ $web_result -eq 0 ]; then
        echo -e "${GREEN}✅ Frontend Tests${NC} - Passed"
    else
        echo -e "${YELLOW}⚠️  Frontend Tests${NC} - Skipped or Failed"
    fi

    if [ $e2e_result -eq 0 ]; then
        echo -e "${GREEN}✅ E2E Tests${NC} - Passed"
    else
        echo -e "${RED}❌ E2E Tests${NC} - Failed"
    fi

    if [ $api_result -eq 0 ] && [ $e2e_result -eq 0 ]; then
        echo ""
        print_success "✅ All critical tests passed!"
        return 0
    else
        echo ""
        print_error "❌ Some tests failed"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Validate OpenAPI spec
validate_openapi() {
    print_header "Validating OpenAPI Specification"

    if ! command -v openapi-generator-cli >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        print_warning "openapi-generator-cli not found, using simple validation"

        if [ -f api/openapi.yaml ]; then
            if grep -q "openapi: 3" api/openapi.yaml; then
                print_success "OpenAPI spec appears valid"
            else
                print_error "OpenAPI spec may be invalid"
                return 1
            fi
        else
            print_error "OpenAPI spec not found at api/openapi.yaml"
            return 1
        fi
    else
        openapi-generator-cli validate -i api/openapi.yaml
    fi
}

# Migrate database between environments
# Usage: db_migrate <src> <dst> [--yes]
db_migrate() {
    local src_env="$1"
    local dst_env="$2"
    local force="$3"

    # Normalize env names
    case "$src_env" in
        staging)  src_env="staging" ;;
        prod|production) src_env="production" ;;
        *)
            print_error "Invalid source environment: $src_env"
            echo "Valid environments: staging, prod"
            return 1
            ;;
    esac

    case "$dst_env" in
        staging)  dst_env="staging" ;;
        prod|production) dst_env="production" ;;
        *)
            print_error "Invalid destination environment: $dst_env"
            echo "Valid environments: staging, prod"
            return 1
            ;;
    esac

    if [ "$src_env" = "$dst_env" ]; then
        print_error "Source and destination cannot be the same environment"
        return 1
    fi

    # Resolve paths for both environments
    local src_path src_domain dst_path dst_domain
    if [ "$src_env" = "staging" ]; then
        src_path="$STAGING_DEPLOY_PATH"
        src_domain="$STAGING_DOMAIN"
    else
        src_path="$PROD_DEPLOY_PATH"
        src_domain="$PROD_DOMAIN"
    fi

    if [ "$dst_env" = "staging" ]; then
        dst_path="$STAGING_DEPLOY_PATH"
        dst_domain="$STAGING_DOMAIN"
    else
        dst_path="$PROD_DEPLOY_PATH"
        dst_domain="$PROD_DOMAIN"
    fi

    print_header "Database Migration"
    echo ""
    echo -e "${CYAN}Source:${NC}      $src_env ($src_domain)"
    echo -e "${CYAN}Destination:${NC} $dst_env ($dst_domain)"
    echo ""

    # Helper: get DB stats for an environment path
    _db_stats() {
        local env_path="$1"
        ssh "$REMOTE_SERVER" "cd '$env_path' && docker compose exec -T -u root api sh -c '
            apk add --no-cache sqlite >/dev/null 2>&1
            for t in \$(sqlite3 /data/sprintspark.db \".tables\"); do
                c=\$(sqlite3 /data/sprintspark.db \"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM \$t;\")
                printf \"  %-25s %s rows\n\" \"\$t\" \"\$c\"
            done
            printf \"  %-25s %s\n\" \"DB size:\" \"\$(du -h /data/sprintspark.db | cut -f1)\"
        '" 2>/dev/null || echo "  (unable to read stats)"
    }

    # Show source DB stats
    print_status "Fetching source database info..."
    echo ""
    echo -e "${CYAN}Source database ($src_env):${NC}"
    _db_stats "$src_path"
    echo ""

    # Show destination DB stats
    print_status "Fetching destination database info..."
    echo -e "${CYAN}Destination database ($dst_env) — WILL BE DESTROYED:${NC}"
    _db_stats "$dst_path"
    echo ""

    # Warning
    echo -e "${RED}╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗${NC}"
    echo -e "${RED}║  ⚠️  WARNING: THIS WILL DESTROY THE $dst_env DATABASE  ${NC}"
    echo -e "${RED}║                                                              ║${NC}"
    echo -e "${RED}║  The $dst_env database at $dst_domain will be${NC}"
    echo -e "${RED}║  completely replaced with a copy from $src_env.${NC}"
    echo -e "${RED}║                                                              ║${NC}"
    echo -e "${RED}║  This action CANNOT be undone.                               ║${NC}"
    echo -e "${RED}╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝${NC}"
    echo ""

    if [ "$force" = "--yes" ]; then
        print_warning "Running in silent mode (--yes) — skipping confirmations"
    else
        # First confirmation
        read -p "Type the destination environment name to confirm ($dst_env): " confirm_env
        if [ "$confirm_env" != "$dst_env" ]; then
            print_status "Migration cancelled"
            return 0
        fi

        # Second confirmation for production
        if [ "$dst_env" = "production" ]; then
            echo ""
            echo -e "${RED}You are about to overwrite the PRODUCTION database!${NC}"
            read -p "Type 'yes-destroy-production' to proceed: " confirm_prod
            if [ "$confirm_prod" != "yes-destroy-production" ]; then
                print_status "Migration cancelled"
                return 0
            fi
        fi
    fi

    echo ""
    print_status "Starting database migration..."

    # Step 1: Create backup of destination database
    print_status "Step 1/5: Backing up destination database..."
    local backup_ts=$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)
    ssh "$REMOTE_SERVER" "cd '$dst_path' && docker compose exec -T api sh -c 'cp /data/sprintspark.db /data/sprintspark.db.backup_${backup_ts}'" || {
        print_error "Failed to backup destination database"
        return 1
    }
    print_success "Backup saved as sprintspark.db.backup_${backup_ts}"

    # Step 2: Export source database to host (checkpoint WAL first for clean copy)
    print_status "Step 2/5: Exporting source database..."
    local src_app_name
    if [ "$src_env" = "staging" ]; then
        src_app_name="sprintspark-staging"
    else
        src_app_name="sprintspark"
    fi
    local src_dir_name
    src_dir_name=$(basename "$src_path")
    local src_volume="${src_dir_name}_${src_app_name}-data"

    # Checkpoint WAL to ensure all data is in the main DB file, then copy
    ssh "$REMOTE_SERVER" "
        cd '$src_path' && docker compose exec -T api sh -c '
            if command -v sqlite3 >/dev/null 2>&1 || apk add --no-cache sqlite >/dev/null 2>&1; then
                sqlite3 /data/sprintspark.db \"PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE);\" >/dev/null 2>&1
            fi
        '
        docker run --rm \
            -v '${src_volume}:/data' \
            -v /tmp:/tmp \
            alpine sh -c 'cp /data/sprintspark.db /tmp/sprintspark_migrate_${backup_ts}.db'
    " || {
        print_error "Failed to export source database"
        return 1
    }

    # Step 3: Stop destination API to prevent writes
    print_status "Step 3/5: Stopping destination API..."
    ssh "$REMOTE_SERVER" "cd '$dst_path' && docker compose stop api" || {
        print_error "Failed to stop destination API"
        return 1
    }

    # Step 4: Import into destination via the Docker volume
    print_status "Step 4/5: Importing database into destination..."
    local dst_dir_name
    dst_dir_name=$(basename "$dst_path")
    # Docker Compose volume name = <project>_<volume> where project = directory name
    # and volume name = <app_name>-data from docker-compose.yml
    local dst_app_name
    if [ "$dst_env" = "staging" ]; then
        dst_app_name="sprintspark-staging"
    else
        dst_app_name="sprintspark"
    fi
    local dst_volume="${dst_dir_name}_${dst_app_name}-data"

    ssh "$REMOTE_SERVER" "
        docker run --rm \
            -v '${dst_volume}:/data' \
            -v /tmp:/tmp \
            alpine sh -c '
                cp /tmp/sprintspark_migrate_${backup_ts}.db /data/sprintspark.db
                rm -f /data/sprintspark.db-wal /data/sprintspark.db-shm
                chmod 666 /data/sprintspark.db
            '
    " || {
        print_error "Failed to import database. Restarting destination..."
        ssh "$REMOTE_SERVER" "cd '$dst_path' && docker compose start api"
        return 1
    }

    # Step 5: Restart destination services
    print_status "Step 5/5: Restarting destination services..."
    ssh "$REMOTE_SERVER" "cd '$dst_path' && docker compose up -d" || {
        print_error "Failed to restart destination services"
        return 1
    }

    # Clean up temp file
    ssh "$REMOTE_SERVER" "rm -f /tmp/sprintspark_migrate_${backup_ts}.db" 2>/dev/null || true

    # Wait and verify health
    print_status "Waiting for services to be healthy..."
    sleep 10

    resolve_env "$dst_env"
    local healthy=false
    for i in $(seq 1 20); do
        if curl -skf "https://$REMOTE_DOMAIN/api/health" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            healthy=true
            break
        fi
        sleep 3
    done

    if [ "$healthy" = true ]; then
        echo ""
        print_success "Database migration completed successfully!"
        echo ""
        echo -e "${CYAN}Source:${NC}      $src_env ($src_domain)"
        echo -e "${CYAN}Destination:${NC} $dst_env ($dst_domain) — now has source data"
        echo -e "${CYAN}Backup:${NC}     sprintspark.db.backup_${backup_ts} (inside destination container)"
        echo ""
        print_status "To restore the backup if needed:"
        echo "  ssh $REMOTE_SERVER"
        echo "  cd $dst_path"
        echo "  docker compose exec api sh -c 'cp /data/sprintspark.db.backup_${backup_ts} /data/sprintspark.db'"
        echo "  docker compose restart api"
    else
        print_error "Migration completed but destination health check failed"
        print_status "Check logs: $0 $( [ '$dst_env' = 'staging' ] && echo 'staging' || echo 'prod' ) logs api"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Execute database query on production
execute_db_query() {
    local query="$1"

    if [ -z "$query" ]; then
        print_error "Usage: $0 db-query \"SQL_QUERY\""
        return 1
    fi

    resolve_env production
    print_header "Production Database Query"
    echo -e "${CYAN}Server:${NC} $REMOTE_SERVER"
    echo -e "${CYAN}Query:${NC} $query"
    echo ""

    # Verify SSH connectivity
    if ! ssh -o ConnectTimeout=10 "$REMOTE_SERVER" "echo ok" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        print_error "Cannot connect to $REMOTE_SERVER"
        return 1
    fi

    # Execute query via docker exec as root (to install sqlite3 if needed)
    ssh "$REMOTE_SERVER" "cd '$REMOTE_DEPLOY_PATH' && docker compose exec -T -u root api sh -c '
        if ! command -v sqlite3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            apk add --no-cache sqlite >/dev/null 2>&1
        fi
        sqlite3 -header -column /data/sprintspark.db \"$query\"
    '"
}

# Admin management - create admin user
# Usage: admin_create <email> <env>
# env: staging, prod/production, local
admin_create() {
    local email="$1"
    local env="${2:-staging}"

    if [ -z "$email" ]; then
        print_error "Usage: $0 [staging|prod] admin create <email>"
        return 1
    fi

    case "$env" in
        staging|prod|production)
            resolve_env "$env"
            print_header "Granting Admin Privileges"
            echo -e "${CYAN}Email:${NC} $email"
            echo -e "${CYAN}Environment:${NC} $ENV_LABEL ($REMOTE_DOMAIN)"
            echo ""

            local result=$(ssh "$REMOTE_SERVER" "cd '$REMOTE_DEPLOY_PATH' && docker compose exec -T -u root api sh -c '
                if ! command -v sqlite3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
                    apk add --no-cache sqlite >/dev/null 2>&1
                fi
                sqlite3 -header -column /data/sprintspark.db \"UPDATE users SET is_admin = 1 WHERE email = '\''$email'\''; SELECT id, email, is_admin FROM users WHERE email = '\''$email'\''\"
            '")
            echo "$result"

            if echo "$result" | grep -q "$email"; then
                echo ""
                print_success "Admin privileges granted to $email on $ENV_LABEL"
            else
                echo ""
                print_error "User $email not found in $ENV_LABEL database. Create the user account first."
                return 1
            fi
            ;;
        local)
            print_header "Granting Admin Privileges (Local)"
            echo -e "${CYAN}Email:${NC} $email"
            echo ""

            if ! docker-compose ps | grep -q "api.*Up"; then
                print_error "Local API container is not running. Start with: $0 local:start"
                return 1
            fi

            local result=$(docker-compose exec -T -u root api sh -c "
                if ! command -v sqlite3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
                    apk add --no-cache sqlite >/dev/null 2>&1
                fi
                sqlite3 -header -column /data/sprintspark.db \"UPDATE users SET is_admin = 1 WHERE email = '$email'; SELECT id, email, is_admin FROM users WHERE email = '$email';\"
            ")
            echo "$result"

            if echo "$result" | grep -q "$email"; then
                echo ""
                print_success "Admin privileges granted to $email (local)"
            else
                echo ""
                print_error "User $email not found in local database. Create the user account first."
                return 1
            fi
            ;;
        *)
            print_error "Invalid environment: $env"
            echo "Valid: staging, prod, local"
            return 1
            ;;
    esac
}

    echo "  demo.user3@sprintspark.app / DemoPass323!"
    echo "  demo.user4@sprintspark.app / DemoPass423!"
    echo "  demo.user5@sprintspark.app / DemoPass523!"
}

# Reset user password (local only, for debugging)
users_reset_password() {
    local email="$1"
    local new_password="${2:-password123}"

    print_header "Reset User Password (LOCAL ONLY - DEBUG)"
    print_warning "⚠️  This command only works in LOCAL environment for debugging"
    echo ""

    if [ -z "$email" ]; then
        print_error "Usage: $0 local users:password <email> [password]"
        echo "Example: $0 local users:password a@b.me MyNewPass123"
        echo ""
        echo "If password is not provided, defaults to: password123"
        return 1
    fi

    if ! docker-compose ps | grep -q "api.*Up"; then
        print_error "Local API container is not running. Start with: $0 local:start"
        return 1
    fi

    print_status "Resetting password for: $email"
    print_status "New password: $new_password"
    echo ""

    # First, check if user exists
    local user_exists=$(docker-compose exec -T -u root api sh -c "
        sqlite3 /data/sprintspark.db \"SELECT id FROM users WHERE email = '$email';\"
    " | tr -d '\r')

    if [ -z "$user_exists" ]; then
        print_error "User $email not found in database"
        return 1
    fi

    # Get user admin status
    local is_admin=$(docker-compose exec -T -u root api sh -c "
        sqlite3 /data/sprintspark.db \"SELECT is_admin FROM users WHERE email = '$email';\"
    " | tr -d '\r')

    print_warning "This will DELETE the user and recreate with new password"
    echo "User ID: $user_exists"
    echo "Admin status: $is_admin"
    echo ""

    # Delete user
    docker-compose exec -T -u root api sh -c "
        sqlite3 /data/sprintspark.db \"DELETE FROM users WHERE email = '$email';\"
    "

    # Recreate via API (which will hash the password)
    local signup_response=$(curl -s -X POST "http://localhost:$LOCAL_API_PORT/api/auth/signup" \
        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
        -d "{\"email\":\"$email\",\"password\":\"$new_password\"}")

    local new_token=$(echo "$signup_response" | python3 -c "import sys, json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('token', ''))" 2>/dev/null || echo "")

    if [ -n "$new_token" ]; then
        # Restore admin status if needed
        if [ "$is_admin" = "1" ]; then
            docker-compose exec -T -u root api sh -c "
                sqlite3 /data/sprintspark.db \"UPDATE users SET is_admin = 1 WHERE email = '$email';\"
            "
            print_status "Restored admin privileges"
        fi

        echo ""
        print_success "Password reset successfully!"
        echo ""
        echo -e "${CYAN}Login credentials:${NC}"
        echo "  Email: $email"
        echo "  Password: $new_password"
    else
        print_error "Failed to reset password"
        echo "Response: $signup_response"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Local development server commands
local_start() {
    print_header "Starting Local Development Server"

    # Check if Docker is running
    if ! docker info >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        print_error "Docker is not running. Please start Docker Desktop first."
        return 1
    fi

    # Check if services are already running
    if docker-compose ps | grep -q "Up"; then
        print_warning "Services are already running"
        docker-compose ps
        return 0
    fi

    print_status "Building and starting services..."
    docker-compose up -d --build

    print_status "Waiting for services to be ready..."
    sleep 5

    # Check health
    local attempts=0
    local max_attempts=10

    while [ $attempts -lt $max_attempts ]; do
        if curl -f -s http://localhost:$LOCAL_API_PORT/api/health >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            print_success "✅ Local server started successfully!"
            echo ""
            echo -e "${CYAN}Local URLs:${NC}"
            echo "  Web:    http://localhost:$LOCAL_WEB_PORT"
            echo "  API:    http://localhost:$LOCAL_API_PORT/api/health"
            echo "  Docs:   http://localhost:$LOCAL_API_PORT/api/openapi"
            echo ""
            print_status "View logs: $0 local:logs"
            return 0
        fi

        attempts=$((attempts + 1))
        sleep 2
    done

    print_error "Services started but not responding. Check logs with: $0 local:logs"
    docker-compose ps
    return 1
}

local_stop() {
    print_header "Stopping Local Development Server"

    if ! docker-compose ps | grep -q "Up"; then
        print_warning "No services are running"
        return 0
    fi

    docker-compose stop
    print_success "Local services stopped"
}

local_restart() {
    print_header "Restarting Local Development Server"

    docker-compose restart

    print_success "Services restarted"
    sleep 3
    local_status
}

local_status() {
    print_header "Local Service Status"

    if ! docker info >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        print_error "Docker is not running"
        return 1
    fi

    docker-compose ps
}

local_logs() {
    local service="${1:-}"

    print_header "Local Logs${service:+: $service}"

    if [ -z "$service" ]; then
        docker-compose logs --tail=50 -f
    else
        docker-compose logs --tail=50 -f "$service"
    fi
}

local_clean() {
    print_header "Cleaning Local Environment"

    print_warning "This will remove all containers, volumes, and data"
    read -p "Are you sure? (y/N) " -n 1 -r
    echo

    if [[ $REPLY =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
        docker-compose down -v
        print_success "Local environment cleaned"
    else
        print_status "Operation cancelled"
    fi
}

local_dev() {
    print_header "Starting Local Development Mode"
    print_status "API will run on http://localhost:8083"
    print_status "Web will run on http://localhost:5173 (Vite dev server)"
    print_status "Changes will auto-reload. Press Ctrl+C to stop."
    echo ""

    # Kill existing processes on these ports
    print_status "Cleaning up existing processes..."
    lsof -ti:8083 | xargs kill -9 2>/dev/null || true
    lsof -ti:5173 | xargs kill -9 2>/dev/null || true

    # Create logs directory
    mkdir -p logs

    # Create trap to cleanup on exit
    trap 'print_warning "Stopping dev servers..."; kill $(jobs -p) 2>/dev/null; exit' INT TERM

    # Start API in background with Air for hot-reload
    print_status "Starting Go API with hot-reload (Air)..."
    cd api
    if ! command -v air &> /dev/null; then
        print_warning "Air not found. Installing..."
        go install github.com/air-verse/air@latest
    fi

    # Create Air config if it doesn't exist
    if [ ! -f .air.toml ]; then
        print_status "Creating Air config..."
        cat > .air.toml << 'EOF'
root = "."
testdata_dir = "testdata"
tmp_dir = "tmp"

[build]
args_bin = []
bin = "./tmp/main"
cmd = "go build -o ./tmp/main ./cmd/api"
delay = 1000
exclude_dir = ["assets", "tmp", "vendor", "testdata"]
exclude_file = []
exclude_regex = ["_test.go"]
exclude_unchanged = false
follow_symlink = false
full_bin = ""
include_dir = []
include_ext = ["go", "tpl", "tmpl", "html"]
include_file = []
kill_delay = "0s"
log = "build-errors.log"
poll = false
poll_interval = 0
post_cmd = []
pre_cmd = []
rerun = false
rerun_delay = 500
send_interrupt = false
stop_on_error = false

[color]
app = ""
build = "yellow"
main = "magenta"
runner = "green"
watcher = "cyan"

[log]
main_only = false
time = false

[misc]
clean_on_exit = false

[screen]
clear_on_rebuild = false
keep_scroll = true
EOF
    fi

    export DB_PATH="../data/sprintspark.db"
    export PORT=8083
    air > ../logs/api.log 2>&1 &
    API_PID=$!
    cd ..

    # Wait a bit for API to start
    sleep 2

    # Start Web dev server in background
    print_status "Starting Vite dev server with hot-reload..."
    cd web
    export VITE_API_URL=http://localhost:8083
    npm run dev > ../logs/web.log 2>&1 &
    WEB_PID=$!
    cd ..

    # Wait for servers to be ready
    sleep 3

    print_success "Development servers started!"
    echo ""
    print_success "✓ API: http://localhost:8083 (Go with Air hot-reload)"
    print_success "✓ Web: http://localhost:5173 (Vite hot-reload)"
    echo ""
    print_status "Logs:"
    print_status "  API: tail -f logs/api.log"
    print_status "  Web: tail -f logs/web.log"
    echo ""
    print_warning "Press Ctrl+C to stop both servers"
    echo ""

    # Tail both logs
    tail -f logs/api.log logs/web.log
}

# Main command handler
case ${1:-help} in
    local:start)
        local_start
        ;;
    local:stop)
        local_stop
        ;;
    local:restart)
        local_restart
        ;;
    local:status)
        local_status
        ;;
    local:logs)
        local_logs "$2"
        ;;
    local:clean)
        local_clean
        ;;
    deploy)
        shift
        deploy_to_staging "$*"
        ;;
    promote)
        promote_to_production "$2"
        ;;
    staging)
        case "$2" in
            status)
                env_status staging
                ;;
            logs)
                env_logs staging "$3"
                ;;
            health)
                env_health staging
                ;;
            restart)
                env_restart staging
                ;;
            stop)
                env_stop staging
                ;;
            start)
                env_start staging
                ;;
            admin)
                case "$3" in
                    create) admin_create "$4" "staging" ;;
                    list)   admin_list "staging" ;;
                    revoke) admin_revoke "$4" "staging" ;;
                    *)
                        print_error "Unknown admin command: $3"
                        echo "Available: create <email>, list, revoke <email>"
                        exit 1
                        ;;
                esac
                ;;
            *)
                print_error "Unknown staging command: $2"
                echo "Available: status, logs, health, restart, stop, start, admin"
                exit 1
                ;;
        esac
        ;;
    prod)
        case "$2" in
            status)
                env_status production
                ;;
            logs)
                env_logs production "$3"
                ;;
            health)
                env_health production
                ;;
            restart)
                env_restart production
                ;;
            stop)
                env_stop production
                ;;
            start)
                env_start production
                ;;
            admin)
                case "$3" in
                    create) admin_create "$4" "production" ;;
                    list)   admin_list "production" ;;
                    revoke) admin_revoke "$4" "production" ;;
                    *)
                        print_error "Unknown admin command: $3"
                        echo "Available: create <email>, list, revoke <email>"
                        exit 1
                        ;;
                esac
                ;;
            *)
                print_error "Unknown production command: $2"
                echo "Available: status, logs, health, restart, stop, start, admin"
                exit 1
                ;;
        esac
        ;;
    # Legacy shortcuts (default to staging for safety)
    restart)
        env_restart staging
        ;;
    stop)
        env_stop staging
        ;;
    start)
        env_start staging
        ;;
    status)
        env_status staging
        ;;
    logs)
        env_logs staging "$2"
        ;;
    health)
        env_health staging
        ;;
    test)
        run_all_tests
        ;;
    test-api)
        test_api
        ;;
    test-web)
        test_web
        ;;
    test-e2e)
        test_e2e
        ;;
    validate-openapi)
        validate_openapi
        ;;
    db-migrate)
        db_migrate "$2" "$3" "$4"
        ;;
    db-query)
        execute_db_query "$2"
        ;;
    admin)
        case "$2" in
            create)
                admin_create "$3" "remote"
                ;;
            list)
                admin_list "remote"
                ;;
            revoke)
                admin_revoke "$3" "remote"
                ;;
            *)
                print_error "Unknown admin command: $2"
                echo "Available: create <email>, list, revoke <email>"
                exit 1
                ;;
        esac
        ;;
    local)
        case "$2" in
            dev)
                local_dev
                ;;
            admin)
                case "$3" in
                    create)
                        admin_create "$4" "local"
                        ;;
                    list)
                        admin_list "local"
                        ;;
                    revoke)
                        admin_revoke "$4" "local"
                        ;;
                    *)
                        print_error "Unknown local admin command: $3"
                        echo "Available: create <email>, list, revoke <email>"
                        exit 1
                        ;;
                esac
                ;;
            users:list)
                users_list_all
                ;;
            users:password)
                users_reset_password "$3" "$4"
                ;;
            *)
                print_error "Unknown local command: $2"
                echo "Available: admin, users:list, users:password"
                exit 1
                ;;
        esac
        ;;
    remote)
        case "$2" in
            admin)
                case "$3" in
                    create)
                        admin_create "$4" "remote"
                        ;;
                    list)
                        admin_list "remote"
                        ;;
                    revoke)
                        admin_revoke "$4" "remote"
                        ;;
                    *)
                        print_error "Unknown remote admin command: $3"
                        echo "Available: create <email>, list, revoke <email>"
                        exit 1
                        ;;
                esac
                ;;
            *)
                print_error "Unknown remote command: $2"
                exit 1
                ;;
        esac
        ;;
    help|--help|-h)
        show_help
        ;;
    *)
        print_error "Unknown command: $1"
        echo ""
        show_help
        exit 1
        ;;
esac

# Multi-Project Webhook Configuration
server:
  port: 9876
  host: "0.0.0.0"
  log_level: "INFO"
  log_file: "/home/ubuntu/webhooks/logs/webhook.log"

# Default settings for all projects
defaults:
  secret: "default-webhook-secret-2025"
  branch: "main"
  timeout: 30
  
# Project configurations
projects:
  nayantara:
    name: "Nayantara's Academic Website"
    repository: "NayantaraB/academic-website"
    secret: "nayantara-website-hook-2025"
    branch: "main"
    local_path: "/home/ubuntu/projects/nayantara-website"
    actions:
      - type: "git_pull"
      - type: "fix_permissions"
        command: "chmod 644 /home/ubuntu/projects/nayantara-website/*"
      - type: "custom_command"
        command: "echo 'Nayantara website updated successfully!'"
    enabled: true
    
  # Example template for future projects
  # example-project:
  #   name: "Example Project"
  #   repository: "user/repository"
  #   secret: "example-secret-2025"
  #   branch: "main"
  #   local_path: "/home/ubuntu/projects/example-project"
  #   actions:
  #     - type: "git_pull"
  #     - type: "build"
  #       command: "npm run build"
  #     - type: "restart_service"
  #       command: "systemctl --user restart example-service"
  #   enabled: false

  sprintspark:
    name: "SprintSpark Project Management System"
    repository: "anchoo2kewl/SprintSpark"
    secret: "sprintspark-webhook-secret-2025"
    branch: "main"
    local_path: "/home/ubuntu/projects/sprintspark"
    actions:
      - type: "git_pull"
      - type: "custom_command"
        command: "cd /home/ubuntu/projects/sprintspark && docker-compose down && docker-compose up --build -d"
      - type: "custom_command"
        command: "echo 'SprintSpark deployed successfully!'"
    enabled: true

# Multi-Project Webhook Configuration
# Copy this file to config.yaml and update with your actual values

server:
  port: 9876
  host: "0.0.0.0"
  log_level: "INFO"
  log_file: "/path/to/webhooks/logs/webhook.log"

# Default settings for all projects
defaults:
  secret: "your-default-webhook-secret"
  branch: "main"
  timeout: 30

# Project configurations
projects:
  # Example project configuration
  example-project:
    name: "Example Project"
    repository: "username/repository"
    secret: "your-webhook-secret-here"
    branch: "main"
    local_path: "/path/to/projects/example-project"
    actions:
      - type: "git_pull"
      - type: "custom_command"
        command: "echo 'Project updated successfully!'"
    enabled: false

  # SprintSpark example configuration
  sprintspark:
    name: "SprintSpark Project Management System"
    repository: "username/SprintSpark"
    secret: "your-sprintspark-webhook-secret"
    branch: "main"
    local_path: "/path/to/projects/sprintspark"
    actions:
      - type: "git_pull"
      - type: "custom_command"
        command: "cd /path/to/projects/sprintspark && docker-compose down && docker-compose up --build -d"
      - type: "custom_command"
        command: "echo 'SprintSpark deployed successfully!'"
    enabled: true

# Available action types:
# - git_pull: Automatically pulls from the repository
# - custom_command: Runs any shell command you specify
# - fix_permissions: Changes file permissions (requires command)

file.content:
# Deploy to Production

Deploy SprintSpark to production server with automated verification.

## Usage

```bash
./script/server deploy [commit_message]
```

## What it does

1. **Commits changes** - Adds all uncommitted files and creates a commit
2. **Pushes to GitHub** - Pushes the main branch to origin
3. **Waits for webhook** - Gives GitHub webhook time to trigger (30s)
4. **Verifies rebuild** - Checks if containers are running, manually rebuilds if needed
5. **Health checks** - Verifies API, Web UI, and database are responding
6. **Reports status** - Shows deployment success with URLs or error details

## Examples

```bash
# Deploy with auto-generated message
./script/server deploy

# Deploy with custom message
./script/server deploy "feat: add new feature"

# Deploy bug fix
./script/server deploy "fix: resolve login issue"
```

## Default Commit Format

If no message provided, uses:
```
Deploy: automated deployment

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
```

## Verification Process

The script performs comprehensive checks:

- ✅ SSH connectivity to production server
- ✅ Container rebuild completion
- ✅ API health endpoint responding
- ✅ Web UI accessible
- ✅ Database connectivity
- ✅ Service health status (up to 20 attempts, 5s intervals)

## Production URLs

After successful deployment:

- **Web**: https://sprintspark.biswas.me
- **API**: https://sprintspark.biswas.me/api/health
- **Docs**: https://sprintspark.biswas.me/api/openapi

## Troubleshooting

If deployment fails:

```bash
# Check container status
./script/server status

# View API logs
./script/server logs api

# View web logs
./script/server logs web

# Manual restart
./script/server restart

# Force rebuild
ssh ubuntu@biswas.me "cd /home/ubuntu/projects/sprintspark && docker-compose down && docker-compose up -d --build"
```

## Related Commands

```bash
# Check health without deploying
./script/server health

# Restart services
./script/server restart

# View real-time logs
./script/server logs api
./script/server logs web

# Check service status
./script/server status

# Query production database
./script/server db-query "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users;"
```

## Deployment Workflow

1. **Local Development**
   ```bash
   # Make changes
   # Test locally
   ./script/server test
   ```

2. **Deploy to Production**
   ```bash
   ./script/server deploy "feat: your feature description"
   ```

3. **Verify Deployment**
   ```bash
   ./script/server health
   ```

## Best Practices

✅ **DO:**
- Test locally before deploying (`./script/server test`)
- Use descriptive commit messages
- Check health after deployment
- Monitor logs for errors

❌ **DON'T:**
- Deploy with failing tests
- Deploy without reviewing changes
- Ignore health check failures
- Skip verification step

## Architecture

```
Local Machine → GitHub → Production Server
     ↓             ↓            ↓
   Commit       Webhook    Auto-rebuild
                            (or manual)
                               ↓
                          Health Check
                               ↓
                        ✅ Deployment OK
```

## Environment

- **Remote Server**: ubuntu@biswas.me
- **Deploy Path**: /home/ubuntu/projects/sprintspark
- **Domain**: sprintspark.biswas.me
- **Services**: Docker Compose (api + web)
- **Deployment**: GitHub webhook or manual trigger

content.content:
1→# Production Management Commands
     2→
     3→Quick reference for managing SprintSpark production environment.
     4→
     5→## Health & Status
     6→
     7→```bash
     8→# Full health check (API, Web, Database)
     9→./script/server health
    10→
    11→# Service status
    12→./script/server status
    13→
    14→# Quick API health
    15→curl https://sprintspark.biswas.me/api/health
    16→```
    17→
    18→## Service Control
    19→
    20→```bash
    21→# Restart all services
    22→./script/server restart
    23→
    24→# Stop services
    25→./script/server stop
    26→
    27→# Start services
    28→./script/server start
    29→```
    30→
    31→## Logs
    32→
    33→```bash
    34→# View API logs (last 50 lines)
    35→./script/server logs api
    36→
    37→# View web logs
    38→./script/server logs web
    39→
    40→# View all logs
    41→./script/server logs
    42→
    43→# Real-time logs
    44→ssh ubuntu@biswas.me "cd /home/ubuntu/projects/sprintspark && docker-compose logs -f api"
    45→```
    46→
    47→## Database Operations
    48→
    49→```bash
    50→# Query users
    51→./script/server db-query "SELECT id, email, is_admin, created_at FROM users ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 10;"
    52→
    53→# Count users
    54→./script/server db-query "SELECT COUNT(*) as total_users FROM users;"
    55→
    56→# View API keys
    57→./script/server db-query "SELECT id, user_id, name, key_prefix, created_at, last_used_at FROM api_keys ORDER BY created_at DESC;"
    58→
    59→# Check projects
    60→./script/server db-query "SELECT id, name, owner_id, created_at FROM projects ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 10;"
    61→
    62→# View tasks
    63→./script/server db-query "SELECT id, title, status, project_id FROM tasks ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 10;"
    64→```
    65→
    66→## Admin Management
    67→
    68→```bash
    69→# List all admins
    70→./script/server admin list
    71→
    72→# Make user admin
    73→./script/server admin create user@example.com
    74→
    75→# Revoke admin
    76→./script/server admin revoke user@example.com
    77→```
    78→
    79→## Quick Troubleshooting
    80→
    81→### API Not Responding
    82→
    83→```bash
    84→# Check logs
    85→./script/server logs api
    86→
    87→# Check container status
    88→./script/server status
    89→
    90→# Restart
    91→./script/server restart
    92→
    93→# Check migration status
    94→./script/server db-query "SELECT version FROM schema_migrations ORDER BY version DESC;"
    95→```
    96→
    97→### Database Issues
    98→
    99→```bash
   100→# Check database file
   101→ssh ubuntu@biswas.me "ls -lh /home/ubuntu/projects/sprintspark/data/sprintspark.db"
   102→
   103→# Check tables
   104→./script/server db-query ".tables"
   105→
   106→# Check schema
   107→./script/server db-query ".schema users"
   108→```
   109→
   110→### Container Issues
   111→
   112→```bash
   113→# SSH into production
   114→ssh ubuntu@biswas.me
   115→
   116→# Navigate to project
   117→cd /home/ubuntu/projects/sprintspark
   118→
   119→# Check containers
   120→docker-compose ps
   121→
   122→# View logs
   123→docker-compose logs --tail=100 api
   124→docker-compose logs --tail=100 web
   125→
   126→# Rebuild
   127→docker-compose down
   128→docker-compose up -d --build
   129→
   130→# Clean restart
   131→docker-compose down -v
   132→docker-compose up -d --build
   133→```
   134→
   135→## Performance Monitoring
   136→
   137→```bash
   138→# Check response time
   139→time curl https://sprintspark.biswas.me/api/health
   140→
   141→# Database size
   142→./script/server db-query "SELECT page_count * page_size as size FROM pragma_page_count(), pragma_page_size();"
   143→
   144→# User activity
   145→./script/server db-query "SELECT COUNT(*) as active_users FROM users WHERE created_at > datetime('now', '-7 days');"
   146→
   147→# Recent activity
   148→./script/server db-query "SELECT action, COUNT(*) as count FROM user_activity WHERE created_at > datetime('now', '-24 hours') GROUP BY action ORDER BY count DESC;"
   149→```
   150→
   151→## Production URLs
   152→
   153→- **Web UI**: https://sprintspark.biswas.me
   154→- **API Health**: https://sprintspark.biswas.me/api/health
   155→- **API Docs**: https://sprintspark.biswas.me/api/openapi
   156→- **Direct API**: https://sprintspark.biswas.me/api
   157→
   158→## Emergency Procedures
   159→
   160→### Complete Service Restart
   161→
   162→```bash
   163→ssh ubuntu@biswas.me "cd /home/ubuntu/projects/sprintspark && docker-compose restart"
   164→```
   165→
   166→### Force Rebuild
   167→
   168→```bash
   169→ssh ubuntu@biswas.me "cd /home/ubuntu/projects/sprintspark && docker-compose down && docker-compose build --no-cache && docker-compose up -d"
   170→```
   171→
   172→### Check Disk Space
   173→
   174→```bash
   175→ssh ubuntu@biswas.me "df -h"
   176→```
   177→
   178→### Clean Docker
   179→
   180→```bash
   181→ssh ubuntu@biswas.me "docker system prune -a --volumes -f"
   182→```
   183→
   184→## Security
   185→
   186→```bash
   187→# Check failed login attempts
   188→./script/server db-query "SELECT action, user_id, created_at FROM user_activity WHERE action LIKE '%failed%' ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 20;"
   189→
   190→# Active API keys

   192→

   194→./script/server db-query "SELECT name, key_prefix, last_used_at FROM api_keys WHERE last_used_at > datetime('now', '-24 hours') ORDER BY last_used_at DESC;"
   195→```

# Production Management Commands

Quick reference for managing SprintSpark production environment.

## Health & Status

```bash
# Full health check (API, Web, Database)
./script/server health

# Service status
./script/server status

# Quick API health
curl https://sprintspark.biswas.me/api/health
```

## Service Control

```bash
# Restart all services
./script/server restart

# Stop services
./script/server stop

# Start services
./script/server start
```

## Logs

```bash
# View API logs (last 50 lines)
./script/server logs api

# View web logs
./script/server logs web

# View all logs
./script/server logs

# Real-time logs
ssh ubuntu@biswas.me "cd /home/ubuntu/projects/sprintspark && docker-compose logs -f api"
```

## Database Operations

```bash
# Query users
./script/server db-query "SELECT id, email, is_admin, created_at FROM users ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 10;"

# Count users
./script/server db-query "SELECT COUNT(*) as total_users FROM users;"

# View API keys
./script/server db-query "SELECT id, user_id, name, key_prefix, created_at, last_used_at FROM api_keys ORDER BY created_at DESC;"

# Check projects
./script/server db-query "SELECT id, name, owner_id, created_at FROM projects ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 10;"

# View tasks
./script/server db-query "SELECT id, title, status, project_id FROM tasks ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 10;"
```

## Admin Management

```bash
# List all admins
./script/server admin list

# Make user admin
./script/server admin create user@example.com

# Revoke admin
./script/server admin revoke user@example.com
```

## Quick Troubleshooting

### API Not Responding

```bash
# Check logs
./script/server logs api

# Check container status
./script/server status

# Restart
./script/server restart

# Check migration status
./script/server db-query "SELECT version FROM schema_migrations ORDER BY version DESC;"
```

### Database Issues

```bash
# Check database file
ssh ubuntu@biswas.me "ls -lh /home/ubuntu/projects/sprintspark/data/sprintspark.db"

# Check tables
./script/server db-query ".tables"

# Check schema
./script/server db-query ".schema users"
```

### Container Issues

```bash
# SSH into production
ssh ubuntu@biswas.me

# Navigate to project
cd /home/ubuntu/projects/sprintspark

# Check containers
docker-compose ps

# View logs
docker-compose logs --tail=100 api
docker-compose logs --tail=100 web

# Rebuild
docker-compose down
docker-compose up -d --build

# Clean restart
docker-compose down -v
docker-compose up -d --build
```

## Performance Monitoring

```bash
# Check response time
time curl https://sprintspark.biswas.me/api/health

# Database size
./script/server db-query "SELECT page_count * page_size as size FROM pragma_page_count(), pragma_page_size();"

# User activity
./script/server db-query "SELECT COUNT(*) as active_users FROM users WHERE created_at > datetime('now', '-7 days');"

# Recent activity
./script/server db-query "SELECT action, COUNT(*) as count FROM user_activity WHERE created_at > datetime('now', '-24 hours') GROUP BY action ORDER BY count DESC;"
```

## Production URLs

- **Web UI**: https://sprintspark.biswas.me
- **API Health**: https://sprintspark.biswas.me/api/health
- **API Docs**: https://sprintspark.biswas.me/api/openapi
- **Direct API**: https://sprintspark.biswas.me/api

## Emergency Procedures

### Complete Service Restart

```bash
ssh ubuntu@biswas.me "cd /home/ubuntu/projects/sprintspark && docker-compose restart"
```

### Force Rebuild

```bash
ssh ubuntu@biswas.me "cd /home/ubuntu/projects/sprintspark && docker-compose down && docker-compose build --no-cache && docker-compose up -d"
```

### Check Disk Space

```bash
ssh ubuntu@biswas.me "df -h"
```

### Clean Docker

```bash
ssh ubuntu@biswas.me "docker system prune -a --volumes -f"
```

## Security

```bash
# Check failed login attempts
./script/server db-query "SELECT action, user_id, created_at FROM user_activity WHERE action LIKE '%failed%' ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 20;"

# Active API keys
./script/server db-query "SELECT COUNT(*) as active_keys FROM api_keys WHERE expires_at IS NULL OR expires_at > datetime('now');"

# Recently used API keys
./script/server db-query "SELECT name, key_prefix, last_used_at FROM api_keys WHERE last_used_at > datetime('now', '-24 hours') ORDER BY last_used_at DESC;"
```

## Server Details

- **Server**: ubuntu@biswas.me
- **Deploy Path**: /home/ubuntu/projects/sprintspark
- **Database**: /home/ubuntu/projects/sprintspark/data/sprintspark.db
- **Docker Compose**: /home/ubuntu/projects/sprintspark/docker-compose.yml
- **Containers**: sprintspark_api_1, sprintspark_web_1

# SSH Access to Production Server

## Server Details
- **Host**: `ubuntu@biswas.me`
- **SprintSpark Project Path**: `/home/ubuntu/projects/sprintspark`

## Common SSH Commands

### Check Container Status
```bash
ssh ubuntu@biswas.me "cd /home/ubuntu/projects/sprintspark && docker compose ps"
```

### View Logs
```bash
# API logs
ssh ubuntu@biswas.me "cd /home/ubuntu/projects/sprintspark && docker compose logs api --tail 50"

# Web logs
ssh ubuntu@biswas.me "cd /home/ubuntu/projects/sprintspark && docker compose logs web --tail 50"

# Follow logs in real-time
ssh ubuntu@biswas.me "cd /home/ubuntu/projects/sprintspark && docker compose logs -f api"
```

### Rebuild and Restart Services
```bash
# Rebuild without cache
ssh ubuntu@biswas.me "cd /home/ubuntu/projects/sprintspark && docker compose build --no-cache"

# Start services
ssh ubuntu@biswas.me "cd /home/ubuntu/projects/sprintspark && docker compose up -d"

# Restart services
ssh ubuntu@biswas.me "cd /home/ubuntu/projects/sprintspark && docker compose restart"

# Stop services
ssh ubuntu@biswas.me "cd /home/ubuntu/projects/sprintspark && docker compose down"
```

### Check Git Status
```bash
ssh ubuntu@biswas.me "cd /home/ubuntu/projects/sprintspark && git log --oneline -5"
ssh ubuntu@biswas.me "cd /home/ubuntu/projects/sprintspark && git status"
```

### Database Operations
```bash
# Access SQLite database
ssh ubuntu@biswas.me "cd /home/ubuntu/projects/sprintspark && docker compose exec api sqlite3 /data/sprintspark.db"

# Run a query
ssh ubuntu@biswas.me "cd /home/ubuntu/projects/sprintspark && docker compose exec api sqlite3 /data/sprintspark.db 'SELECT * FROM swim_lanes LIMIT 5;'"
```

### Health Checks
```bash
# Check API health
curl -s https://sprintspark.biswas.me/api/health

# Check healthz endpoint
curl -s https://sprintspark.biswas.me/healthz

# Check web UI
curl -I https://sprintspark.biswas.me
```

## Deployment Workflow

### Full Deployment Process

Use this complete workflow to deploy changes from local to production:

1. **Build locally** (if needed)
   ```bash
   # For frontend changes
   cd web && npm run build

   # For backend changes
   cd api && go build ./cmd/api
   ```

2. **Commit and push changes**
   ```bash
   git add -A
   git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
   <type>(<scope>): <subject>

   - Detailed change 1
   - Detailed change 2

   🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

   Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
   EOF
   )"
   git push origin main
   ```

3. **Pull latest code on production**
   ```bash
   ssh ubuntu@biswas.me "cd /home/ubuntu/projects/sprintspark && git pull origin main"
   ```

4. **Rebuild affected containers**
   ```bash
   # For API changes
   ssh ubuntu@biswas.me "cd /home/ubuntu/projects/sprintspark && docker compose build api"

   # For web changes
   ssh ubuntu@biswas.me "cd /home/ubuntu/projects/sprintspark && docker compose build web"

   # For both or unclear changes
   ssh ubuntu@biswas.me "cd /home/ubuntu/projects/sprintspark && docker compose build --no-cache"
   ```

5. **Restart services**
   ```bash
   ssh ubuntu@biswas.me "cd /home/ubuntu/projects/sprintspark && docker compose up -d"
   ```

6. **Verify deployment**
   ```bash
   # Check container health
   ssh ubuntu@biswas.me "cd /home/ubuntu/projects/sprintspark && docker compose ps"

   # Check API logs
   ssh ubuntu@biswas.me "cd /home/ubuntu/projects/sprintspark && docker compose logs api --tail 30"

   # Test health endpoints
   curl -s https://sprintspark.biswas.me/api/health
   curl -I https://sprintspark.biswas.me
   ```

### Quick Deploy (when webhook fails)

When webhook deployment fails, use this streamlined process:

```bash
# 1. Check status
ssh ubuntu@biswas.me "cd /home/ubuntu/projects/sprintspark && docker compose ps"

# 2. Pull, rebuild, and restart
ssh ubuntu@biswas.me "cd /home/ubuntu/projects/sprintspark && git pull origin main && docker compose build --no-cache && docker compose up -d"

# 3. Verify
ssh ubuntu@biswas.me "cd /home/ubuntu/projects/sprintspark && docker compose ps"
curl -s https://sprintspark.biswas.me/api/health
```

## Troubleshooting

### Containers not running
```bash
# Check logs for errors
ssh ubuntu@biswas.me "cd /home/ubuntu/projects/sprintspark && docker compose logs --tail 100"

# Try rebuilding
ssh ubuntu@biswas.me "cd /home/ubuntu/projects/sprintspark && docker compose down && docker compose build --no-cache && docker compose up -d"
```

### Migrations not applied
- Migrations run automatically on API startup
- Check logs to confirm: `docker compose logs api | grep -i migration`
- Migrations are located in `/app/internal/db/migrations` inside the container

### 502 Bad Gateway
- Usually means containers aren't running
- Check with: `docker compose ps`
- Restart with: `docker compose restart`

## Webhook Information

The webhook server runs on the production server and automatically:
1. Pulls latest code from GitHub when commits are pushed to `main`
2. Rebuilds Docker containers
3. Restarts services

**Webhook health**: https://webhook.biswas.me/health

If webhook deployment fails, use the manual deployment workflow above.

1→# SprintSpark ⚡
     2→
     3→[![CI](https://github.com/anchoo2kewl/SprintSpark/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/anchoo2kewl/SprintSpark/actions/workflows/ci.yml)
     4→[![Go Report Card](https://goreportcard.com/badge/github.com/anchoo2kewl/SprintSpark)](https://goreportcard.com/report/github.com/anchoo2kewl/SprintSpark)
     5→[![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-blue.svg)](LICENSE)
     6→
     7→> A lightweight, production-grade project management system built with Go, SQLite, React, and TypeScript.
     8→
     9→**Philosophy:** Small, perfect commits > large, broken features
    10→
    11→---
    12→
    13→## ⚡ Quick Start (< 3 commands)
    14→
    15→```bash
    16→git clone https://github.com/anchoo2kewl/SprintSpark.git
    17→cd SprintSpark
    18→docker-compose up --build
    19→```
    20→
    21→**That's it!** Visit [http://localhost](http://localhost) 🎉
    22→
    23→---
    24→
    25→## 📋 Table of Contents
    26→
    27→- [Features](#-features)
    28→- [Architecture](#-architecture)
    29→- [Getting Started](#-getting-started)
    30→- [API Documentation](#-api-documentation)
    31→- [Testing](#-testing)
    32→- [Deployment](#-deployment)
    33→- [Contributing](#-contributing)
    34→- [Troubleshooting](#-troubleshooting)
    35→- [License](#-license)
    36→
    37→---
    38→
    39→## ✨ Features
    40→
    41→### Core Functionality
    42→- 📊 **Project Management** - Create, organize, and track multiple projects
    43→- ✅ **Task Tracking** - Search, filter, and manage tasks with rich metadata
    44→- 🔐 **Authentication** - Secure JWT-based auth with bcrypt password hashing
    45→- 🔍 **Full-Text Search** - Find tasks and projects instantly
    46→- 📱 **Responsive UI** - Works on desktop and mobile
    47→
    48→### Technical Features
    49→- 🚀 **RESTful API** - Complete OpenAPI 3.1 specification
    50→- 🎨 **Modern Stack** - React 18 + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS
    51→- 💾 **SQLite Database** - Zero-config, portable, reliable
    52→- 🐳 **Docker Ready** - Multi-stage builds for production
    53→- 🔄 **Auto-Deploy** - GitHub webhook integration
    54→- ✅ **Type-Safe** - End-to-end TypeScript with generated API types
    55→- 🧪 **Well-Tested** - Unit tests + E2E tests with Playwright
    56→- 📊 **CI/CD** - GitHub Actions with security scanning
    57→
    58→---
    59→
    60→## 🏗️ Architecture
    61→
    62→```
    63→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    64→│                     Client Browser                      │
    65→│                    (React + TypeScript)                 │
    66→└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────┘
    67→                     │ HTTPS
    68→                     ▼
    69→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    70→│                   Nginx (Port 80/443)                   │
    71→│              (Gzip, Security Headers, SPA)              │
    72→└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────┘
    73→                     │
    74→                     ▼
    75→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    76→│                  Go API (Port 8080)                     │
    77→│         (JWT Auth, OpenAPI, Rate Limiting)              │
    78→└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────┘
    79→                     │
    80→                     ▼
    81→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    82→│                   SQLite Database                       │
    83→│            (Migrations, Foreign Keys, WAL)              │
    84→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    85→```
    86→
    87→### Component Breakdown
    88→
    89→| Component | Technology | Purpose |
    90→|-----------|-----------|---------|
    91→| **Frontend** | React 18, TypeScript, Vite, Tailwind CSS | SPA with type-safe API client |
    92→| **Backend** | Go 1.24, Chi router, JWT | RESTful API with authentication |
    93→| **Database** | SQLite 3 (WAL mode) | Embedded database with migrations |
    94→| **Testing** | Playwright, Go testing | E2E and unit tests |
    95→| **CI/CD** | GitHub Actions, Docker | Automated testing and deployment |
    96→| **Deployment** | Docker Compose, Nginx | Production containers |
    97→
    98→---
    99→
   100→## 🚀 Getting Started
   101→
   102→### Prerequisites
   103→
   104→**Option 1: Docker (Recommended)**
   105→- Docker Desktop or Docker Engine 20+
   106→- Docker Compose v2+
   107→
   108→**Option 2: Local Development**
   109→- Go 1.24+
   110→- Node.js 20+
   111→- npm 10+
   112→
   113→### Installation & Setup
   114→
   115→#### **Option 1: Docker (Production Build)**
   116→
   117→```bash
   118→# Clone the repository
   119→git clone https://github.com/anchoo2kewl/SprintSpark.git
   120→cd SprintSpark
   121→
   122→# Start all services
   123→docker-compose up --build -d
   124→
   125→# Check status
   126→docker-compose ps
   127→```
   128→
   129→Access the application:
   130→- **Frontend:** http://localhost
   131→- **API:** http://localhost:8080
   132→- **API Docs:** http://localhost:8080/api/openapi
   133→
   134→#### **Option 2: Local Development (Hot Reload)**
   135→
   136→```bash
   137→# Clone the repository
   138→git clone https://github.com/anchoo2kewl/SprintSpark.git
   139→cd SprintSpark
   140→
   141→# Start everything with hot-reload
   142→make dev
   143→```
   144→
   145→This starts:
   146→- API with Air hot-reload on http://localhost:8080
   147→- Web with Vite HMR on http://localhost:5173
   148→
   149→#### **Option 3: Manual Setup**
   150→
   151→**Backend:**
   152→```bash
   153→cd api
   154→
   155→# Copy environment file
   156→cp .env.example .env
   157→
   158→# Install dependencies
   159→go mod download
   160→
   161→# Run migrations
   162→make migrate
   163→
   164→# Start API server
   165→make run
   166→```
   167→
   168→**Frontend:**
   169→```bash
   170→cd web
   171→
   172→# Copy environment file
   173→cp .env.example .env
   174→
   175→# Install dependencies
   176→npm install
   177→
   178→# Generate TypeScript types from OpenAPI
   179→npm run generate:types
   180→
   181→# Start dev server
   182→npm run dev
   183→```
   184→
   185→### First Steps
   186→
   187→1. **Create an account** - Navigate to http://localhost:5173/signup
   188→2. **Login** - Use your credentials
   189→3. **Create a project** - Click "New Project"
   190→4. **Add tasks** - Start tracking your work!
   191→
   192→---
   193→
   194→## 📚 API Documentation
   195→
   196→### Interactive Documentation
   197→
   198→The OpenAPI specification is available at:
   199→- **JSON:** http://localhost:8080/api/openapi
