    24→// JWTAuth middleware validates JWT tokens or API keys from Authorization header
    25→func (s *Server) JWTAuth(next http.Handler) http.Handler {
    26→	return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
    27→		// Extract token from Authorization header
    28→		authHeader := r.Header.Get("Authorization")
    29→		if authHeader == "" {
    30→			respondError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "missing authorization header", "unauthorized")
    31→			return
    32→		}
    33→
    34→		// Check for Bearer token format
    35→		parts := strings.SplitN(authHeader, " ", 2)
    36→		if len(parts) != 2 {
    37→			respondError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "invalid authorization header format", "unauthorized")
    38→			return
    39→		}
    40→
    41→		authType := parts[0]
    42→		credential := parts[1]
    43→
    44→		var userID int64
    45→		var email string
    46→		var err error
    47→
    48→		switch authType {
    49→		case "Bearer":
    50→			// JWT token authentication
    51→			claims, jwtErr := auth.ValidateToken(credential, s.config.JWTSecret)
    52→			if jwtErr != nil {
    53→				log.Printf("Token validation failed: %v", jwtErr)
    54→				respondError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "invalid or expired token", "unauthorized")
    55→				return
    56→			}
    57→			userID = claims.UserID
    58→			email = claims.Email
    59→
    60→		case "ApiKey":
    61→			// API key authentication
    62→			userID, email, err = s.db.GetUserByAPIKey(r.Context(), credential)
    63→			if err != nil {
    64→				log.Printf("API key validation failed: %v", err)
    65→				respondError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "invalid or expired API key", "unauthorized")
    66→				return
    67→			}
    68→
    69→		default:
    70→			respondError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "unsupported authorization type", "unauthorized")
    71→			return
    72→		}
    73→
    74→		// Add user info to request context
    75→		ctx := context.WithValue(r.Context(), UserIDKey, userID)
    76→		ctx = context.WithValue(ctx, UserEmailKey, email)
    77→
    78→		// Continue to next handler
    79→		next.ServeHTTP(w, r.WithContext(ctx))
    80→	})
    81→}

    83→// Logger middleware logs HTTP requests
    84→func Logger(next http.Handler) http.Handler {
    85→	return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
    86→		start := time.Now()
    87→
    88→		// Wrap response writer to capture status code
    89→		wrapped := &responseWriter{ResponseWriter: w, statusCode: http.StatusOK}
    90→
    91→		next.ServeHTTP(wrapped, r)
    92→
    93→		log.Printf(
    94→			"%s %s %d %s",
    95→			r.Method,
    96→			r.URL.Path,
    97→			wrapped.statusCode,
    98→			time.Since(start),
    99→		)
   100→	})
   101→}
   102→
   103→// responseWriter wraps http.ResponseWriter to capture status code
   104→type responseWriter struct {
   105→	http.ResponseWriter
   106→	statusCode int
   107→}
   108→
   109→func (rw *responseWriter) WriteHeader(code int) {
   110→	rw.statusCode = code
   111→	rw.ResponseWriter.WriteHeader(code)
   112→}

   114→// GetUserID extracts user ID from request context
   115→func GetUserID(r *http.Request) (int64, bool) {

   117→	return userID, ok
   118→}

   126→// tokenBucket implements a simple token bucket rate limiter
   127→type tokenBucket struct {
   128→	tokens      float64
   129→	capacity    float64
   130→	refillRate  float64
   131→	lastRefill  time.Time
   132→	mu          sync.Mutex
   133→}
   134→
   135→func newTokenBucket(capacity, refillRate float64) *tokenBucket {
   136→	return &tokenBucket{
   137→		tokens:     capacity,
   138→		capacity:   capacity,
   139→		refillRate: refillRate,
   140→		lastRefill: time.Now(),
   141→	}
   142→}
   143→
   144→func (tb *tokenBucket) allow() bool {
   145→	tb.mu.Lock()
   146→	defer tb.mu.Unlock()
   147→
   148→	now := time.Now()
   149→	elapsed := now.Sub(tb.lastRefill).Seconds()
   150→
   151→	// Refill tokens based on elapsed time
   152→	tb.tokens += elapsed * tb.refillRate
   153→	if tb.tokens > tb.capacity {
   154→		tb.tokens = tb.capacity
   155→	}
   156→	tb.lastRefill = now
   157→
   158→	// Check if we have tokens available
   159→	if tb.tokens >= 1.0 {
   160→		tb.tokens -= 1.0
   161→		return true
   162→	}
   163→
   164→	return false
   165→}
   166→
   167→// rateLimiter manages rate limits for different endpoints
   168→type rateLimiter struct {
   169→	buckets map[string]*tokenBucket
   170→	mu      sync.RWMutex
   171→}
   172→
   173→func newRateLimiter() *rateLimiter {
   174→	return &rateLimiter{
   175→		buckets: make(map[string]*tokenBucket),
   176→	}
   177→}
   178→
   179→func (rl *rateLimiter) getBucket(key string, capacity, refillRate float64) *tokenBucket {
   180→	rl.mu.RLock()
   181→	bucket, exists := rl.buckets[key]
   182→	rl.mu.RUnlock()
   183→
   184→	if exists {
   185→		return bucket
   186→	}
   187→
   188→	// Create new bucket
   189→	rl.mu.Lock()
   190→	defer rl.mu.Unlock()
   191→
   192→	// Double-check after acquiring write lock
   193→	bucket, exists = rl.buckets[key]
   194→	if exists {
   195→		return bucket
   196→	}
   197→
   198→	bucket = newTokenBucket(capacity, refillRate)
   199→	rl.buckets[key] = bucket
   200→	return bucket
   201→}
   202→
   203→// RateLimitMiddleware creates a rate limiting middleware
   204→func RateLimitMiddleware(requestsPerMinute int) func(http.Handler) http.Handler {
   205→	limiter := newRateLimiter()
   206→	capacity := float64(requestsPerMinute)
   207→	refillRate := capacity / 60.0 // tokens per second
   208→
   209→	return func(next http.Handler) http.Handler {
   210→		return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   211→			// Use IP address as key
   212→			ip := r.RemoteAddr
   213→			if forwarded := r.Header.Get("X-Forwarded-For"); forwarded != "" {
   214→				ip = strings.Split(forwarded, ",")[0]
   215→			}
   216→			if realIP := r.Header.Get("X-Real-IP"); realIP != "" {
   217→				ip = realIP
   218→			}
   219→
   220→			bucket := limiter.getBucket(ip, capacity, refillRate)
   221→			if !bucket.allow() {
   222→				respondError(w, http.StatusTooManyRequests, "rate limit exceeded", "rate_limit_exceeded")
   223→				return
   224→			}
   225→
   226→			next.ServeHTTP(w, r)
   227→		})
   228→	}
   229→}

   125→// MakeRequest is a helper to make HTTP requests in tests
   126→// Returns both the ResponseRecorder and the Request for testing
   127→func MakeRequest(t *testing.T, method, path string, body interface{}, headers map[string]string) (*httptest.ResponseRecorder, *http.Request) {
   128→	t.Helper()
   129→
   130→	var reqBody []byte
   131→	var err error
   132→	if body != nil {
   133→		reqBody, err = json.Marshal(body)
   134→		if err != nil {
   135→			t.Fatalf("Failed to marshal request body: %v", err)
   136→		}
   137→	}
   138→
   139→	req := httptest.NewRequest(method, path, bytes.NewBuffer(reqBody))
   140→	req.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
   141→
   142→	for key, value := range headers {
   143→		req.Header.Set(key, value)
   144→	}
   145→
   146→	return httptest.NewRecorder(), req
   147→}

   159→func AssertStatusCode(t *testing.T, got, want int) {

   162→	if got != want {
   163→		t.Errorf("Status code mismatch: got %d, want %d", got, want)
   164→	}
   165→}

   167→// AssertJSONField checks if a JSON field has the expected value
   168→func AssertJSONField(t *testing.T, data map[string]interface{}, field string, want interface{}) {
   169→	t.Helper()
   170→
   171→	got, ok := data[field]
   172→	if !ok {
   173→		t.Errorf("Field %q not found in response", field)
   174→		return
   175→	}
   176→
   177→	if got != want {
   178→		t.Errorf("Field %q mismatch: got %v, want %v", field, got, want)
   179→	}
   180→}

   260→// MakeAuthRequest creates an HTTP request with auth context (UserIDKey) and optional chi URL params
   261→func (ts *TestServer) MakeAuthRequest(t *testing.T, method, path string, body interface{}, userID int64, urlParams map[string]string) (*httptest.ResponseRecorder, *http.Request) {
   262→	t.Helper()
   263→
   264→	rec, req := MakeRequest(t, method, path, body, nil)
   265→
   266→	ctx := context.WithValue(req.Context(), UserIDKey, userID)
   267→
   268→	if len(urlParams) > 0 {
   269→		rctx := chi.NewRouteContext()
   270→		for k, v := range urlParams {
   271→			rctx.URLParams.Add(k, v)
   272→		}
   273→		ctx = context.WithValue(ctx, chi.RouteCtxKey, rctx)
   274→	}
   275→
   276→	req = req.WithContext(ctx)
   277→	return rec, req
   278→}

   280→// AssertError checks if the error response matches expected error and code
   281→func AssertError(t *testing.T, rec *httptest.ResponseRecorder, wantCode int, wantErrorContains, wantCodeContains string) {
   282→	t.Helper()
   283→
   284→	AssertStatusCode(t, rec.Code, wantCode)
   285→
   286→	var errResp ErrorResponse
   287→	DecodeJSON(t, rec, &errResp)
   288→
   289→	if wantErrorContains != "" && !contains(errResp.Error, wantErrorContains) {
   290→		t.Errorf("Error message %q does not contain %q", errResp.Error, wantErrorContains)
   291→	}
   292→
   293→	if wantCodeContains != "" && !contains(errResp.Code, wantCodeContains) {
   294→		t.Errorf("Error code %q does not contain %q", errResp.Code, wantCodeContains)
   295→	}
   296→}
   297→
   298→// contains checks if a string contains a substring
   299→func contains(s, substr string) bool {
   300→	return len(s) >= len(substr) && (s == substr || indexOf(s, substr) >= 0)
   301→}
   302→
   303→// indexOf returns the index of the first occurrence of substr in s, or -1
   304→func indexOf(s, substr string) int {
   305→	for i := 0; i <= len(s)-len(substr); i++ {
   306→		if s[i:i+len(substr)] == substr {
   307→			return i
   308→		}
   309→	}
   310→	return -1
   311→}

   298→		if err != nil {

   470→func TestGetClientIP(t *testing.T) {
   471→	tests := []struct {
   472→		name   string
   473→		xff    string
   474→		xri    string
   475→		remote string
   476→		want   string
   477→	}{
   478→		{"X-Forwarded-For single", "1.2.3.4", "", "5.6.7.8:1234", "1.2.3.4"},
   479→		{"X-Forwarded-For multiple", "1.2.3.4, 10.0.0.1", "", "5.6.7.8:1234", "1.2.3.4"},
   480→		{"X-Real-IP", "", "9.8.7.6", "5.6.7.8:1234", "9.8.7.6"},
   481→		{"RemoteAddr fallback", "", "", "5.6.7.8:1234", "5.6.7.8:1234"},
   482→	}
   483→
   484→	for _, tt := range tests {
   485→		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
   486→			req, _ := http.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, "/", nil)
   487→			if tt.xff != "" {
   488→				req.Header.Set("X-Forwarded-For", tt.xff)
   489→			}
   490→			if tt.xri != "" {
   491→				req.Header.Set("X-Real-IP", tt.xri)
   492→			}
   493→			req.RemoteAddr = tt.remote
   494→
   495→			got := getClientIP(req)
   496→			if got != tt.want {
   497→				t.Errorf("getClientIP() = %q, want %q", got, tt.want)
   498→			}
   499→		})
   500→	}
   501→}

   140→func TestHandleDeleteAPIKey(t *testing.T) {
   141→	tests := []struct {
   142→		name          string
   143→		setupFunc     func(*TestServer) (userID int64, keyID string)
   144→		wantStatus    int
   145→		wantError     string
   146→		wantErrorCode string
   147→		noAuth        bool
   148→	}{
   149→		{
   150→			name: "delete own API key successfully",
   151→			setupFunc: func(ts *TestServer) (int64, string) {
   152→				userID := ts.CreateTestUser(t, "user@example.com", "password123")
   153→				apiKey, err := ts.DB.CreateAPIKey(context.Background(), userID, "My Key", nil)
   154→				if err != nil {
   155→					t.Fatalf("Failed to create API key: %v", err)
   156→				}
   157→				return userID, fmt.Sprintf("%d", apiKey.ID)
   158→			},
   159→			wantStatus: http.StatusNoContent,
   160→		},
   161→		{
   162→			name: "delete non-existent key returns not found",
   163→			setupFunc: func(ts *TestServer) (int64, string) {
   164→				userID := ts.CreateTestUser(t, "user@example.com", "password123")
   165→				return userID, "99999"
   166→			},
   167→			wantStatus:    http.StatusNotFound,
   168→			wantError:     "API key not found",
   169→			wantErrorCode: "not_found",
   170→		},
   171→		{
   172→			name: "cannot delete another user's key",
   173→			setupFunc: func(ts *TestServer) (int64, string) {
   174→				ownerID := ts.CreateTestUser(t, "owner@example.com", "password123")
   175→				apiKey, err := ts.DB.CreateAPIKey(context.Background(), ownerID, "Owner Key", nil)
   176→				if err != nil {
   177→					t.Fatalf("Failed to create API key: %v", err)
   178→				}
   179→				// Return a different user as the requester
   180→				otherUserID := ts.CreateTestUser(t, "other@example.com", "password123")
   181→				return otherUserID, fmt.Sprintf("%d", apiKey.ID)
   182→			},
   183→			wantStatus:    http.StatusNotFound,
   184→			wantError:     "API key not found",
   185→			wantErrorCode: "not_found",
   186→		},
   187→		{
   188→			name: "invalid key ID format",
   189→			setupFunc: func(ts *TestServer) (int64, string) {
   190→				userID := ts.CreateTestUser(t, "user@example.com", "password123")
   191→				return userID, "not-a-number"
   192→			},
   193→			wantStatus:    http.StatusBadRequest,
   194→			wantError:     "invalid API key ID",
   195→			wantErrorCode: "invalid_request",
   196→		},
   197→		{
   198→			name:          "unauthenticated request",
   199→			noAuth:        true,
   200→			wantStatus:    http.StatusUnauthorized,
   201→			wantError:     "unauthorized",
   202→			wantErrorCode: "unauthorized",
   203→		},
   204→	}
   205→
   206→	for _, tt := range tests {
   207→		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
   208→			ts := NewTestServer(t)
   209→			defer ts.Close()
   210→
   211→			if tt.noAuth {
   212→				rec, req := MakeRequest(t, http.MethodDelete, "/api/api-keys/1", nil, nil)
   213→				ts.HandleDeleteAPIKey(rec, req)
   214→
   215→				AssertStatusCode(t, rec.Code, tt.wantStatus)
   216→				if tt.wantError != "" {
   217→					AssertError(t, rec, tt.wantStatus, tt.wantError, tt.wantErrorCode)
   218→				}
   219→				return
   220→			}
   221→
   222→			userID, keyIDStr := tt.setupFunc(ts)
   223→
   224→			urlParams := map[string]string{"id": keyIDStr}
   225→			rec, req := ts.MakeAuthRequest(t, http.MethodDelete, "/api/api-keys/"+keyIDStr, nil, userID, urlParams)
   226→			ts.HandleDeleteAPIKey(rec, req)
   227→
   228→			AssertStatusCode(t, rec.Code, tt.wantStatus)
   229→
   230→			if tt.wantError != "" {
   231→				AssertError(t, rec, tt.wantStatus, tt.wantError, tt.wantErrorCode)
   232→			}
   233→
   234→			// For successful delete, verify the key is actually gone
   235→			if tt.wantStatus == http.StatusNoContent {
   236→				keys, err := ts.DB.GetAPIKeysByUserID(context.Background(), userID)
   237→				if err != nil {
   238→					t.Fatalf("Failed to list API keys: %v", err)
   239→				}
   240→				if len(keys) != 0 {
   241→					t.Errorf("Expected 0 API keys after delete, got %d", len(keys))
   242→				}
   243→			}
   244→		})
   245→	}
   246→}

    25→// TestServer represents an in-process test server
    26→type TestServer struct {
    27→	Server  *http.Server
    28→	BaseURL string

    30→	Client  *http.Client
    31→	cleanup func()

    34→// NewTestServer creates a new test server with in-memory database
    35→func NewTestServer(t *testing.T) *TestServer {
    36→	t.Helper()
    37→
    38→	// Create temporary database
    39→	tmpDB := fmt.Sprintf(":memory:")
    40→
    41→	// Create server configuration
    42→	cfg := &config.Config{
    43→		Port:               "0", // Random port
    44→		Env:                "test",
    45→		DBPath:             tmpDB,
    46→		MigrationsPath:     "../db/migrations",
    47→		JWTSecret:          "test-secret-key-for-e2e-tests",
    48→		JWTExpiryHours:     24,
    49→		CORSAllowedOrigins: []string{"*"},
    50→		LogLevel:           "error",
    51→	}
    52→
    53→	// Initialize logger
    54→	logger := config.MustInitLogger(cfg.Env, cfg.LogLevel)
    55→
    56→	// Initialize database
    57→	dbCfg := db.Config{
    58→		DBPath:         tmpDB,
    59→		MigrationsPath: "../db/migrations",
    60→	}
    61→
    62→	database, err := db.New(dbCfg, logger)
    63→	if err != nil {
    64→		t.Fatalf("Failed to create database: %v", err)
    65→	}
    66→
    67→	// Create API server
    68→	server := api.NewServer(database, cfg, logger)
    69→
    70→	// Setup router
    71→	r := chi.NewRouter()
    72→
    73→	// Middleware stack
    74→	r.Use(middleware.RequestID)
    75→	r.Use(middleware.RealIP)
    76→	r.Use(middleware.Recoverer)
    77→	r.Use(middleware.Timeout(30 * time.Second))
    78→
    79→	// CORS configuration for tests
    80→	r.Use(cors.Handler(cors.Options{
    81→		AllowedOrigins:   []string{"*"},
    82→		AllowedMethods:   []string{"GET", "POST", "PUT", "PATCH", "DELETE", "OPTIONS"},
    83→		AllowedHeaders:   []string{"Accept", "Authorization", "Content-Type"},
    84→		ExposedHeaders:   []string{"Link"},
    85→		AllowCredentials: true,
    86→		MaxAge:           300,
    87→	}))
    88→
    89→	// Public routes
    90→	r.Get("/", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
    91→		w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
    92→		w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
    93→		fmt.Fprintf(w, `{"message":"TaskAI API","version":"0.1.0"}`)
    94→	})
    95→
    96→	r.Get("/healthz", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
    97→		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(r.Context(), 2*time.Second)
    98→		defer cancel()
    99→
   100→		if err := database.HealthCheck(ctx); err != nil {
   101→			w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
   102→			w.WriteHeader(http.StatusServiceUnavailable)
   103→			fmt.Fprintf(w, `{"status":"error","message":"database unavailable"}`)
   104→			return
   105→		}
   106→
   107→		w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
   108→		w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
   109→		fmt.Fprintf(w, `{"status":"ok","database":"connected"}`)
   110→	})
   111→
   112→	// API routes
   113→	r.Route("/api", func(r chi.Router) {
   114→		r.Get("/openapi", server.HandleOpenAPI)
   115→
   116→		// Auth routes (public)
   117→		r.Route("/auth", func(r chi.Router) {
   118→			r.Post("/signup", server.HandleSignup)
   119→			r.Post("/login", server.HandleLogin)
   120→		})
   121→
   122→		// Protected routes
   123→		r.Group(func(r chi.Router) {
   124→			r.Use(server.JWTAuth)
   125→
   126→			r.Get("/me", server.HandleMe)
   127→
   128→			// Project routes
   129→			r.Get("/projects", server.HandleListProjects)
   130→			r.Post("/projects", server.HandleCreateProject)
   131→			r.Get("/projects/{id}", server.HandleGetProject)
   132→			r.Patch("/projects/{id}", server.HandleUpdateProject)
   133→			r.Delete("/projects/{id}", server.HandleDeleteProject)
   134→
   135→			// Task routes
   136→			r.Get("/projects/{projectId}/tasks", server.HandleListTasks)
   137→			r.Post("/projects/{projectId}/tasks", server.HandleCreateTask)
   138→			r.Patch("/tasks/{id}", server.HandleUpdateTask)
   139→			r.Delete("/tasks/{id}", server.HandleDeleteTask)
   140→
   141→			// Team routes
   142→			r.Route("/team", func(r chi.Router) {
   143→				r.Get("/", server.HandleGetMyTeam)
   144→				r.Get("/members", server.HandleGetTeamMembers)
   145→				r.Post("/invitations", server.HandleInviteTeamMember)
   146→				r.Get("/invitations", server.HandleGetMyInvitations)
   147→				r.Post("/invitations/{id}/accept", server.HandleAcceptInvitation)
   148→				r.Post("/invitations/{id}/reject", server.HandleRejectInvitation)
   149→				r.Delete("/members/{memberId}", server.HandleRemoveTeamMember)
   150→			})
   151→		})
   152→	})
   153→
   154→	// Find available port
   155→	listener, err := net.Listen("tcp", ":0")
   156→	if err != nil {
   157→		t.Fatalf("Failed to find available port: %v", err)
   158→	}
   159→	port := listener.Addr().(*net.TCPAddr).Port
   160→	listener.Close()
   161→
   162→	// Create HTTP server
   163→	httpServer := &http.Server{
   164→		Addr:    fmt.Sprintf(":%d", port),
   165→		Handler: r,
   166→	}
   167→
   168→	// Start server in background
   169→	go func() {
   170→		if err := httpServer.ListenAndServe(); err != nil && err != http.ErrServerClosed {
   171→			t.Logf("Server error: %v", err)
   172→		}
   173→	}()
   174→
   175→	// Wait for server to be ready
   176→	baseURL := fmt.Sprintf("http://localhost:%d", port)
   177→	client := &http.Client{Timeout: 10 * time.Second}
   178→	
   179→	for i := 0; i < 50; i++ {
   180→		resp, err := client.Get(baseURL + "/healthz")
   181→		if err == nil {
   182→			resp.Body.Close()
   183→			if resp.StatusCode == http.StatusOK {
   184→				break
   185→			}
   186→		}
   187→		time.Sleep(10 * time.Millisecond)
   188→	}
   189→
   190→	cleanup := func() {
   191→		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)
   192→		defer cancel()
   193→		httpServer.Shutdown(ctx)
   194→		database.Close()
   195→	}
   196→
   197→	return &TestServer{
   198→		Server:  httpServer,
   199→		BaseURL: baseURL,
   200→		DB:      database.DB,
   201→		Client:  client,
   202→		cleanup: cleanup,
   203→	}
   204→}
   205→
   206→// Close cleans up the test server
   207→func (ts *TestServer) Close() {
   208→	if ts.cleanup != nil {
   209→		ts.cleanup()
   210→	}
   211→}
   212→
   213→// Helper functions for API calls
   214→
   215→// DoRequest makes an HTTP request and returns the response
   216→func (ts *TestServer) DoRequest(method, path string, body interface{}, token string) (*http.Response, error) {
   217→	var bodyReader io.Reader
   218→	if body != nil {
   219→		jsonBody, err := json.Marshal(body)
   220→		if err != nil {
   221→			return nil, err
   222→		}
   223→		bodyReader = bytes.NewBuffer(jsonBody)
   224→	}
   225→
   226→	req, err := http.NewRequest(method, ts.BaseURL+path, bodyReader)
   227→	if err != nil {
   228→		return nil, err
   229→	}
   230→
   231→	req.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
   232→	if token != "" {
   233→		req.Header.Set("Authorization", "Bearer "+token)
   234→	}
   235→
   236→	return ts.Client.Do(req)
   237→}
   238→
   239→// ParseJSON parses JSON response
   240→func ParseJSON(resp *http.Response, v interface{}) error {
   241→	defer resp.Body.Close()
   242→	return json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(v)
   243→}
   244→
   245→// Signup creates a new user
   246→func (ts *TestServer) Signup(email, password string) (string, map[string]interface{}, error) {
   247→	body := map[string]string{
   248→		"email":    email,
   249→		"password": password,
   250→	}
   251→
   252→	resp, err := ts.DoRequest("POST", "/api/auth/signup", body, "")
   253→	if err != nil {
   254→		return "", nil, err
   255→	}
   256→
   257→	if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusCreated {
   258→		return "", nil, fmt.Errorf("signup failed with status %d", resp.StatusCode)
   259→	}
   260→
   261→	var result map[string]interface{}
   262→	if err := ParseJSON(resp, &result); err != nil {
   263→		return "", nil, err
   264→	}
   265→
   266→	token, ok := result["token"].(string)
   267→	if !ok {
   268→		return "", nil, fmt.Errorf("no token in response")
   269→	}
   270→
   271→	return token, result, nil
   272→}

   290→	var result map[string]interface{}

   295→	token, ok := result["token"].(string)
   296→	if !ok {

   776→	project, err := ts.CreateProject(user1Token, "Shared Project", "Should be visible to team members")
   777→	if err != nil {

   782→	inviteBody := map[string]string{"email": user2Email}
   783→	inviteResp, err := ts.DoRequest("POST", "/api/team/invitations", inviteBody, user1Token)
   784→	if err != nil {

    42→	if err != nil {
    43→		logger.Fatal("Failed to initialize database", zap.Error(err))
    44→	}
    45→	defer database.Close()

    48→	server := api.NewServer(database, cfg, logger)

    50→	// Setup router
    51→	r := chi.NewRouter()
    52→
    53→	// Middleware stack
    54→	r.Use(middleware.RequestID)
    55→	r.Use(middleware.RealIP)
    56→	r.Use(api.Logger)
    57→	r.Use(middleware.Recoverer)
    58→	r.Use(middleware.Timeout(30 * time.Second))
    59→
    60→	// Request size limit (1MB)
    61→	r.Use(middleware.SetHeader("Content-Type", "application/json"))
    62→	r.Use(func(next http.Handler) http.Handler {
    63→		return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
    64→			r.Body = http.MaxBytesReader(w, r.Body, 1<<20) // 1MB
    65→			next.ServeHTTP(w, r)
    66→		})
    67→	})
    68→
    69→	// CORS configuration
    70→	r.Use(cors.Handler(cors.Options{
    71→		AllowedOrigins:   cfg.CORSAllowedOrigins,
    72→		AllowedMethods:   []string{"GET", "POST", "PUT", "PATCH", "DELETE", "OPTIONS"},
    73→		AllowedHeaders:   []string{"Accept", "Authorization", "Content-Type"},
    74→		ExposedHeaders:   []string{"Link"},

    83→		fmt.Fprintf(w, `{"message":"TaskAI API","version":"0.1.0"}`)
    84→	})

   227→			// Image library
   228→			r.Get("/images", server.HandleListImages)
   229→
   230→			// Asset management
   231→			r.Get("/assets", server.HandleListAssets)
   232→
   233→			// Attachment update and delete
   234→			r.Patch("/attachments/{id}", server.HandleUpdateAttachment)
   235→			r.Delete("/attachments/{id}", server.HandleDeleteAttachment)
   236→
   237→			// Invite routes
   238→			r.Get("/invites", server.HandleListInvites)
   239→			r.Post("/invites", server.HandleCreateInvite)
   240→
   241→			// Admin routes (requires admin role)
   242→			r.Get("/admin/users", server.HandleGetUsers)
   243→			r.Get("/admin/users/{id}/activity", server.HandleGetUserActivity)
   244→			r.Patch("/admin/users/{id}/admin", server.HandleUpdateUserAdmin)
   245→			r.Patch("/admin/users/{id}/invites", server.HandleAdminBoostInvites)
   246→
   247→			// Admin email provider routes
   248→			r.Get("/admin/settings/email", server.HandleGetEmailProvider)
   249→			r.Post("/admin/settings/email", server.HandleSaveEmailProvider)
   250→			r.Delete("/admin/settings/email", server.HandleDeleteEmailProvider)
   251→			r.Post("/admin/settings/email/test", server.HandleTestEmailProvider)
   252→		})
   253→	})
   254→
   255→	// Start background health checks
   256→	bgCtx, bgCancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
   257→	defer bgCancel()
   258→	server.StartBrevoHealthCheck(bgCtx)
   259→
   260→	// Create HTTP server
   261→	addr := fmt.Sprintf(":%s", cfg.Port)
   262→	srv := &http.Server{
   263→		Addr:         addr,
   264→		Handler:      r,
   265→		ReadTimeout:  15 * time.Second,
   266→		WriteTimeout: 15 * time.Second,
   267→		IdleTimeout:  60 * time.Second,
   268→	}
   269→
   270→	// Channel to listen for server errors
   271→	serverErrors := make(chan error, 1)
   272→
   273→	// Start server in goroutine
   274→	go func() {
   275→		logger.Info("Server listening", zap.String("addr", addr))
   276→		serverErrors <- srv.ListenAndServe()
   277→	}()
   278→
   279→	// Channel to listen for interrupt signals
   280→	shutdown := make(chan os.Signal, 1)
   281→	signal.Notify(shutdown, os.Interrupt, syscall.SIGTERM)
   282→
   283→	// Block until we receive shutdown signal or server error
   284→	select {
   285→	case err := <-serverErrors:
   286→		logger.Fatal("Server error", zap.Error(err))
   287→	case sig := <-shutdown:
   288→		logger.Info("Received shutdown signal, starting graceful shutdown", zap.String("signal", sig.String()))
   289→
   290→		// Give outstanding requests 30 seconds to complete
   291→		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 30*time.Second)
   292→		defer cancel()
   293→
   294→		// Gracefully shutdown server
   295→		if err := srv.Shutdown(ctx); err != nil {
   296→			logger.Error("Graceful shutdown failed", zap.Error(err))
   297→			if err := srv.Close(); err != nil {
   298→				logger.Fatal("Failed to close server", zap.Error(err))
   299→			}
   300→		}
   301→
   302→		logger.Info("Server stopped gracefully")
   303→	}
   304→}
   305→"}]},"uuid":"4e8a215f-5803-40b1-a9ab-6257a50e262f","timestamp":"2026-02-17T23:25:19.896Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"b8f4831e-8368-4eea-b138-5c2c7d93f065"}

    15→// Server holds the application dependencies
    16→type Server struct {
    17→	db           *db.DB
    18→	config       *config.Config
    19→	logger       *zap.Logger
    20→	emailService *email.BrevoService
    21→	emailMu      sync.RWMutex
    22→}
    23→
    24→// NewServer creates a new API server
    25→func NewServer(database *db.DB, cfg *config.Config, logger *zap.Logger) *Server {
    26→	return &Server{
    27→		db:     database,
    28→		config: cfg,
    29→		logger: logger,
    30→	}
    31→}
    32→
    33→// getAppURL returns the application URL for use in email links
    34→func (s *Server) getAppURL() string {
    35→	if len(s.config.CORSAllowedOrigins) > 0 {
    36→		return s.config.CORSAllowedOrigins[0]
    37→	}
    38→	return "http://localhost:5173"
    39→}
    40→
    41→// invalidateEmailService clears the cached email service so it's reloaded on next use
    42→func (s *Server) invalidateEmailService() {
    43→	s.emailMu.Lock()
    44→	defer s.emailMu.Unlock()
    45→	s.emailService = nil
    46→}
    47→
    48→// GetEmailService returns the email service, loading from DB if needed. Returns nil if not configured.
    49→func (s *Server) GetEmailService() *email.BrevoService {
    50→	s.emailMu.RLock()
    51→	if s.emailService != nil {
    52→		svc := s.emailService
    53→		s.emailMu.RUnlock()
    54→		return svc
    55→	}
    56→	s.emailMu.RUnlock()
    57→
    58→	// Load from DB
    59→	s.emailMu.Lock()
    60→	defer s.emailMu.Unlock()
    61→
    62→	// Double-check after acquiring write lock
    63→	if s.emailService != nil {
    64→		return s.emailService
    65→	}
    66→
    67→	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)
    68→	defer cancel()
    69→
    70→	var apiKey, senderEmail, senderName, status string
    71→	err := s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
    72→		`SELECT api_key, sender_email, sender_name, status FROM email_provider WHERE id = 1`,
    73→	).Scan(&apiKey, &senderEmail, &senderName, &status)
    74→	if err != nil {
    75→		return nil
    76→	}
    77→
    78→	if status == "suspended" {
    79→		return nil
    80→	}
    81→
    82→	s.emailService = email.NewBrevoService(apiKey, senderEmail, senderName, s.logger)
    83→	return s.emailService
    84→}
    85→"}]},"uuid":"083a90f5-b14f-4cc8-a87c-38f690c9886a","timestamp":"2026-02-17T23:25:20.328Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"d761b5c9-b908-400a-9a2f-e81fb04f7ee3"}

{"parentUuid":"6433b7c0-2a50-4a3c-9896-b8ff45be5ca4","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/anshumanbiswas/play/SprintSpark","sessionId":"70515e69-241b-4985-b5d3-6390746eba7b","version":"2.1.44","gitBranch":"main","agentId":"a774bf5","slug":"steady-dancing-pelican","type":"progress","data":{"type":"hook_progress","hookEvent":"PostToolUse","hookName":"PostToolUse:Read","command":"callback"},"parentToolUseID":"REDACTED","toolUseID":"REDACTED","timestamp":"2026-02-17T23:25:20.759Z","uuid":"12a911d7-1d8a-4d76-b710-2bd02af1a8af"}
{"parentUuid":"6433b7c0-2a50-4a3c-9896-b8ff45be5ca4","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/anshumanbiswas/play/SprintSpark","sessionId":"70515e69-241b-4985-b5d3-6390746eba7b","version":"2.1.44","gitBranch":"main","agentId":"a774bf5","slug":"steady-dancing-pelican","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"REDACTED","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→package api
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"database/sql"
     6→	"fmt"
     7→
     8→	"net/http"
     9→	"strings"
    10→	"time"
    11→
    12→	"go.uber.org/zap"
    13→
    14→	"sprintspark/internal/auth"
    15→)
    16→
    17→// SignupRequest represents the signup request payload
    18→type SignupRequest struct {
    19→	Email      string `json:"email"`
    20→	Password   string `json:"password"`
    21→	InviteCode string `json:"invite_code"`
    22→}
    23→
    24→// LoginRequest represents the login request payload
    25→type LoginRequest struct {
    26→	Email    string `json:"email"`
    27→	Password string `json:"password"`
    28→}
    29→
    30→// AuthResponse represents the authentication response
    31→type AuthResponse struct {
    32→	Token string `json:"token"`
    33→	User  User   `json:"user"`
    34→}
    35→
    36→// User represents a user
    37→type User struct {
    38→	ID        int64     `json:"id"`
    39→	Email     string    `json:"email"`
    40→	Name      string    `json:"name,omitempty"`
    41→	IsAdmin   bool      `json:"is_admin"`
    42→	CreatedAt time.Time `json:"created_at"`
    43→}
    44→
    45→// HandleSignup creates a new user account
    46→func (s *Server) HandleSignup(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
    47→	var req SignupRequest
    48→	if err := decodeJSON(r, &req); err != nil {
    49→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "invalid request body", "invalid_request")
    50→		return
    51→	}
    52→
    53→	// Validate input
    54→	if err := validateSignupRequest(req); err != nil {
    55→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, err.Error(), "validation_error")
    56→		return
    57→	}
    58→
    59→	// Validate invite code
    60→	if req.InviteCode == "" {
    61→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "invite code is required — you need a referral to create an account", "invite_required")
    62→		return
    63→	}
    64→
    65→	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(r.Context(), 10*time.Second)
    66→	defer cancel()
    67→
    68→	// Verify invite code is valid
    69→	var inviteID int64
    70→	var usedAt sql.NullString
    71→	var expiresAt sql.NullString
    72→	err := s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
    73→		`SELECT id, used_at, expires_at FROM invites WHERE code = ?`, req.InviteCode,
    74→	).Scan(&inviteID, &usedAt, &expiresAt)
    75→	if err == sql.ErrNoRows {
    76→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "invalid invite code", "invalid_invite")
    77→		return
    78→	}
    79→	if err != nil {
    80→		s.logger.Error("Failed to validate invite code", zap.Error(err))
    81→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to create user", "internal_error")
    82→		return
    83→	}
    84→	if usedAt.Valid {
    85→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "this invite has already been used", "invite_used")
    86→		return
    87→	}
    88→	if expiresAt.Valid {
    89→		t, parseErr := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, expiresAt.String)
    90→		if parseErr != nil {
    91→			t, parseErr = time.Parse("2006-01-02 15:04:05-07:00", expiresAt.String)
    92→		}
    93→		if parseErr == nil && time.Now().After(t) {
    94→			respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "this invite has expired", "invite_expired")
    95→			return
    96→		}
    97→	}
    98→
    99→	// Hash password
   100→	hashedPassword, err := auth.HashPassword(req.Password)
   101→	if err != nil {
   102→		s.logger.Error("Failed to hash password", zap.Error(err))
   103→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to create user", "internal_error")
   104→		return
   105→	}
   106→
   107→	tx, err := s.db.BeginTx(ctx, nil)
   108→	if err != nil {
   109→		s.logger.Error("Failed to begin transaction", zap.Error(err))
   110→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to create user", "internal_error")
   111→		return
   112→	}
   113→	defer tx.Rollback()
   114→
   115→	// Create user
   116→	userQuery := `
   117→		INSERT INTO users (email, password_hash)
   118→		VALUES (?, ?)
   119→		RETURNING id, email, name, is_admin, created_at
   120→	`
   121→
   122→	var user User
   123→	var name sql.NullString
   124→	err = tx.QueryRowContext(ctx, userQuery, req.Email, hashedPassword).
   125→		Scan(&user.ID, &user.Email, &name, &user.IsAdmin, &user.CreatedAt)
   126→
   127→	if name.Valid {
   128→		user.Name = name.String
   129→	}
   130→
   131→	if err != nil {
   132→		if strings.Contains(err.Error(), "UNIQUE constraint failed") {
   133→			respondError(w, http.StatusConflict, "email already exists", "email_exists")
   134→			return
   135→		}
   136→		s.logger.Error("Failed to create user", zap.Error(err), zap.String("email", req.Email))
   137→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to create user", "internal_error")
   138→		return
   139→	}
   140→
   141→	// Create team for the user
   142→	teamName := user.Email + "'s Team"
   143→	if user.Name != "" {
   144→		teamName = user.Name + "'s Team"
   145→	}
   146→
   147→	teamQuery := `
   148→		INSERT INTO teams (name, owner_id, created_at, updated_at)
   149→		VALUES (?, ?, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP)
   150→	`
   151→	teamResult, err := tx.ExecContext(ctx, teamQuery, teamName, user.ID)
   152→	if err != nil {
   153→		s.logger.Error("Failed to create team", zap.Error(err), zap.Int64("user_id", user.ID))
   154→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to create team", "internal_error")
   155→		return
   156→	}
   157→
   158→	teamID, err := teamResult.LastInsertId()
   159→	if err != nil {
   160→		s.logger.Error("Failed to get team ID", zap.Error(err))
   161→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to create team", "internal_error")
   162→		return
   163→	}
   164→
   165→	// Add user to team as owner
   166→	memberQuery := `
   167→		INSERT INTO team_members (team_id, user_id, role, status, joined_at)
   168→		VALUES (?, ?, 'owner', 'active', CURRENT_TIMESTAMP)
   169→	`
   170→	_, err = tx.ExecContext(ctx, memberQuery, teamID, user.ID)
   171→	if err != nil {
   172→		s.logger.Error("Failed to add user to team", zap.Error(err), zap.Int64("user_id", user.ID))
   173→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to add user to team", "internal_error")
   174→		return
   175→	}
   176→
   177→	// Mark invite as used
   178→	_, err = tx.ExecContext(ctx,
   179→		`UPDATE invites SET invitee_id = ?, used_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP WHERE id = ?`,
   180→		user.ID, inviteID,
   181→	)
   182→	if err != nil {
   183→		s.logger.Error("Failed to mark invite as used", zap.Error(err))
   184→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to create user", "internal_error")
   185→		return
   186→	}
   187→
   188→	// Commit transaction
   189→	if err := tx.Commit(); err != nil {
   190→		s.logger.Error("Failed to commit transaction", zap.Error(err))
   191→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to create user", "internal_error")
   192→		return
   193→	}
   194→
   195→	s.logger.Info("User and team created",
   196→		zap.Int64("user_id", user.ID),
   197→		zap.Int64("team_id", teamID),
   198→		zap.String("email", user.Email),
   199→	)
   200→
   201→	// Generate JWT token
   202→	token, err := auth.GenerateToken(user.ID, user.Email, s.config.JWTSecret, s.config.JWTExpiry())
   203→	if err != nil {
   204→		s.logger.Error("Failed to generate token", zap.Error(err))
   205→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to generate token", "internal_error")
   206→		return
   207→	}
   208→
   209→	respondJSON(w, http.StatusCreated, AuthResponse{
   210→		Token: token,
   211→		User:  user,
   212→	})
   213→}
   214→
   215→// HandleLogin authenticates a user and returns a JWT token
   216→func (s *Server) HandleLogin(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   217→	var req LoginRequest
   218→	if err := decodeJSON(r, &req); err != nil {
   219→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "invalid request body", "invalid_request")
   220→		return
   221→	}
   222→
   223→	// Validate input
   224→	if req.Email == "" || req.Password == "" {
   225→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "email and password are required", "validation_error")
   226→		return
   227→	}
   228→
   229→	// Get user from database
   230→	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(r.Context(), 5*time.Second)
   231→	defer cancel()
   232→
   233→	query := `SELECT id, email, name, password_hash, is_admin, created_at FROM users WHERE email = ?`
   234→
   235→	var user User
   236→	var passwordHash string
   237→	var name sql.NullString
   238→	err := s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, query, req.Email).
   239→		Scan(&user.ID, &user.Email, &name, &passwordHash, &user.IsAdmin, &user.CreatedAt)
   240→
   241→	if name.Valid {
   242→		user.Name = name.String
   243→	}
   244→
   245→	if err != nil {
   246→		if err == sql.ErrNoRows {
   247→			// Log failed login attempt
   248→			respondError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "invalid email or password", "invalid_credentials")
   249→			return
   250→		}
   251→		s.logger.Error("Failed to query user", zap.Error(err))
   252→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to authenticate", "internal_error")
   253→		return
   254→	}
   255→
   256→	// Verify password
   257→	if err := auth.VerifyPassword(passwordHash, req.Password); err != nil {
   258→		// Log failed login attempt
   259→		go s.logUserActivity(context.Background(), user.ID, "failed_login", getClientIP(r), r.UserAgent())
   260→		respondError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "invalid email or password", "invalid_credentials")
   261→		return
   262→	}
   263→
   264→	// Log successful login
   265→	go s.logUserActivity(context.Background(), user.ID, "login", getClientIP(r), r.UserAgent())
   266→
   267→	// Generate JWT token
   268→	token, err := auth.GenerateToken(user.ID, user.Email, s.config.JWTSecret, s.config.JWTExpiry())
   269→	if err != nil {
   270→		s.logger.Error("Failed to generate token", zap.Error(err))
   271→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to generate token", "internal_error")
   272→		return
   273→	}
   274→
   275→	respondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, AuthResponse{
   276→		Token: token,
   277→		User:  user,
   278→	})
   279→}
   280→
   281→// HandleMe returns the current authenticated user
   282→func (s *Server) HandleMe(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   283→	userID, ok := GetUserID(r)
   284→	if !ok {
   285→		respondError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "user not authenticated", "unauthorized")
   286→		return
   287→	}
   288→
   289→	// Get user from database
   290→	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(r.Context(), 5*time.Second)
   291→	defer cancel()
   292→
   293→	query := `SELECT id, email, name, is_admin, created_at FROM users WHERE id = ?`
   294→
   295→	var user User
   296→	var name sql.NullString
   297→	err := s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, query, userID).
   298→		Scan(&user.ID, &user.Email, &name, &user.IsAdmin, &user.CreatedAt)
   299→
   300→	if name.Valid {
   301→		user.Name = name.String
   302→	}
   303→
   304→	if err != nil {
   305→		if err == sql.ErrNoRows {
   306→			respondError(w, http.StatusNotFound, "user not found", "not_found")
   307→			return
   308→		}
   309→		s.logger.Error("Failed to query user", zap.Error(err))
   310→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to get user", "internal_error")
   311→		return
   312→	}
   313→
   314→	respondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, user)
   315→}

   317→// UpdateProfileRequest represents the update profile request
   318→type UpdateProfileRequest struct {
   319→	Name string `json:"name"`
   320→}
   321→
   322→// HandleUpdateProfile updates the current user's profile
   323→func (s *Server) HandleUpdateProfile(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   324→	userID, ok := GetUserID(r)
   325→	if !ok {
   326→		respondError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "user not authenticated", "unauthorized")
   327→		return
   328→	}
   329→
   330→	var req UpdateProfileRequest
   331→	if err := decodeJSON(r, &req); err != nil {
   332→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "invalid request body", "invalid_request")
   333→		return
   334→	}
   335→
   336→	// Validate name
   337→	if len(req.Name) > 100 {
   338→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "name must be 100 characters or less", "validation_error")
   339→		return
   340→	}
   341→
   342→	// Update user name
   343→	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(r.Context(), 5*time.Second)
   344→	defer cancel()
   345→
   346→	query := `UPDATE users SET name = ? WHERE id = ?`
   347→	_, err := s.db.ExecContext(ctx, query, req.Name, userID)
   348→	if err != nil {
   349→		s.logger.Error("Failed to update user profile", zap.Error(err))
   350→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to update profile", "internal_error")
   351→		return
   352→	}
   353→
   354→	// Get updated user
   355→	userQuery := `SELECT id, email, name, is_admin, created_at FROM users WHERE id = ?`
   356→	var user User
   357→	var name sql.NullString
   358→	err = s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, userQuery, userID).
   359→		Scan(&user.ID, &user.Email, &name, &user.IsAdmin, &user.CreatedAt)
   360→
   361→	if name.Valid {
   362→		user.Name = name.String
   363→	}
   364→
   365→	if err != nil {
   366→		s.logger.Error("Failed to query user", zap.Error(err))
   367→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to get user", "internal_error")
   368→		return
   369→	}
   370→
   371→	respondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, user)
   372→}

   374→// validateSignupRequest validates the signup request
   375→func validateSignupRequest(req SignupRequest) error {
   376→	// Validate email
   377→	if req.Email == "" {
   378→		return fmt.Errorf("email is required")
   379→	}
   380→	if !strings.Contains(req.Email, "@") || !strings.Contains(req.Email, ".") {
   381→		return fmt.Errorf("invalid email format")
   382→	}
   383→
   384→	// Validate password strength
   385→	if err := validatePasswordStrength(req.Password); err != nil {
   386→		return err
   387→	}
   388→
   389→	return nil
   390→}
   391→
   392→// validatePasswordStrength ensures password meets security requirements
   393→func validatePasswordStrength(password string) error {
   394→	if password == "" {
   395→		return fmt.Errorf("password is required")
   396→	}
   397→
   398→	if len(password) < 8 {
   399→		return fmt.Errorf("password must be at least 8 characters")
   400→	}
   401→
   402→	// Check for at least one digit
   403→	hasDigit := false
   404→	for _, ch := range password {
   405→		if ch >= '0' && ch <= '9' {
   406→			hasDigit = true
   407→			break
   408→		}
   409→	}
   410→	if !hasDigit {
   411→		return fmt.Errorf("password must contain at least one digit")
   412→	}
   413→
   414→	// Check for at least one letter (uppercase or lowercase)
   415→	hasLetter := false
   416→	for _, ch := range password {
   417→		if (ch >= 'a' && ch <= 'z') || (ch >= 'A' && ch <= 'Z') {
   418→			hasLetter = true
   419→			break
   420→		}
   421→	}
   422→	if !hasLetter {
   423→		return fmt.Errorf("password must contain at least one letter")
   424→	}
   425→
   426→	return nil
   427→}

    14→// contextKey is a custom type for context keys to avoid collisions
    15→type contextKey string
    16→
    17→const (
    18→	// UserIDKey is the context key for user ID
    19→	UserIDKey contextKey = "user_id"
    20→	// UserEmailKey is the context key for user email
    21→	UserEmailKey contextKey = "user_email"

    23→
    24→// JWTAuth middleware validates JWT tokens or API keys from Authorization header
    25→func (s *Server) JWTAuth(next http.Handler) http.Handler {
    26→	return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
    27→		// Extract token from Authorization header
    28→		authHeader := r.Header.Get("Authorization")
    29→		if authHeader == "" {
    30→			respondError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "missing authorization header", "unauthorized")
    31→			return
    32→		}
    33→
    34→		// Check for Bearer token format
    35→		parts := strings.SplitN(authHeader, " ", 2)
    36→		if len(parts) != 2 {
    37→			respondError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "invalid authorization header format", "unauthorized")
    38→			return
    39→		}
    40→
    41→		authType := parts[0]
    42→		credential := parts[1]
    43→
    44→		var userID int64
    45→		var email string
    46→		var err error
    47→
    48→		switch authType {
    49→		case "Bearer":
    50→			// JWT token authentication
    51→			claims, jwtErr := auth.ValidateToken(credential, s.config.JWTSecret)
    52→			if jwtErr != nil {
    53→				log.Printf("Token validation failed: %v", jwtErr)
    54→				respondError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "invalid or expired token", "unauthorized")
    55→				return
    56→			}
    57→			userID = claims.UserID
    58→			email = claims.Email
    59→
    60→		case "ApiKey":
    61→			// API key authentication
    62→			userID, email, err = s.db.GetUserByAPIKey(r.Context(), credential)
    63→			if err != nil {
    64→				log.Printf("API key validation failed: %v", err)
    65→				respondError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "invalid or expired API key", "unauthorized")
    66→				return
    67→			}
    68→
    69→		default:
    70→			respondError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "unsupported authorization type", "unauthorized")
    71→			return
    72→		}
    73→
    74→		// Add user info to request context
    75→		ctx := context.WithValue(r.Context(), UserIDKey, userID)
    76→		ctx = context.WithValue(ctx, UserEmailKey, email)
    77→
    78→		// Continue to next handler
    79→		next.ServeHTTP(w, r.WithContext(ctx))
    80→	})
    81→}
    82→
    83→// Logger middleware logs HTTP requests
    84→func Logger(next http.Handler) http.Handler {
    85→	return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
    86→		start := time.Now()
    87→
    88→		// Wrap response writer to capture status code
    89→		wrapped := &responseWriter{ResponseWriter: w, statusCode: http.StatusOK}
    90→
    91→		next.ServeHTTP(wrapped, r)
    92→
    93→		log.Printf(
    94→			"%s %s %d %s",
    95→			r.Method,
    96→			r.URL.Path,
    97→			wrapped.statusCode,
    98→			time.Since(start),
    99→		)
   100→	})
   101→}
   102→
   103→// responseWriter wraps http.ResponseWriter to capture status code
   104→type responseWriter struct {
   105→	http.ResponseWriter
   106→	statusCode int
   107→}
   108→
   109→func (rw *responseWriter) WriteHeader(code int) {
   110→	rw.statusCode = code
   111→	rw.ResponseWriter.WriteHeader(code)
   112→}

   114→// GetUserID extracts user ID from request context
   115→func GetUserID(r *http.Request) (int64, bool) {

   117→	return userID, ok
   118→}
   119→
   120→// GetUserEmail extracts user email from request context
   121→func GetUserEmail(r *http.Request) (string, bool) {
   122→	email, ok := r.Context().Value(UserEmailKey).(string)
   123→	return email, ok
   124→}

   126→// tokenBucket implements a simple token bucket rate limiter
   127→type tokenBucket struct {
   128→	tokens      float64
   129→	capacity    float64
   130→	refillRate  float64
   131→	lastRefill  time.Time
   132→	mu          sync.Mutex
   133→}
   134→
   135→func newTokenBucket(capacity, refillRate float64) *tokenBucket {
   136→	return &tokenBucket{
   137→		tokens:     capacity,
   138→		capacity:   capacity,
   139→		refillRate: refillRate,
   140→		lastRefill: time.Now(),
   141→	}
   142→}
   143→
   144→func (tb *tokenBucket) allow() bool {
   145→	tb.mu.Lock()
   146→	defer tb.mu.Unlock()
   147→
   148→	now := time.Now()
   149→	elapsed := now.Sub(tb.lastRefill).Seconds()
   150→
   151→	// Refill tokens based on elapsed time
   152→	tb.tokens += elapsed * tb.refillRate
   153→	if tb.tokens > tb.capacity {
   154→		tb.tokens = tb.capacity
   155→	}
   156→	tb.lastRefill = now
   157→
   158→	// Check if we have tokens available
   159→	if tb.tokens >= 1.0 {
   160→		tb.tokens -= 1.0
   161→		return true
   162→	}
   163→
   164→	return false
   165→}
   166→
   167→// rateLimiter manages rate limits for different endpoints
   168→type rateLimiter struct {
   169→	buckets map[string]*tokenBucket
   170→	mu      sync.RWMutex
   171→}
   172→
   173→func newRateLimiter() *rateLimiter {
   174→	return &rateLimiter{
   175→		buckets: make(map[string]*tokenBucket),
   176→	}
   177→}
   178→
   179→func (rl *rateLimiter) getBucket(key string, capacity, refillRate float64) *tokenBucket {
   180→	rl.mu.RLock()
   181→	bucket, exists := rl.buckets[key]
   182→	rl.mu.RUnlock()
   183→
   184→	if exists {
   185→		return bucket
   186→	}
   187→
   188→	// Create new bucket
   189→	rl.mu.Lock()
   190→	defer rl.mu.Unlock()
   191→
   192→	// Double-check after acquiring write lock
   193→	bucket, exists = rl.buckets[key]
   194→	if exists {
   195→		return bucket
   196→	}
   197→
   198→	bucket = newTokenBucket(capacity, refillRate)
   199→	rl.buckets[key] = bucket
   200→	return bucket
   201→}
   202→
   203→// RateLimitMiddleware creates a rate limiting middleware
   204→func RateLimitMiddleware(requestsPerMinute int) func(http.Handler) http.Handler {
   205→	limiter := newRateLimiter()
   206→	capacity := float64(requestsPerMinute)
   207→	refillRate := capacity / 60.0 // tokens per second
   208→
   209→	return func(next http.Handler) http.Handler {
   210→		return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   211→			// Use IP address as key
   212→			ip := r.RemoteAddr
   213→			if forwarded := r.Header.Get("X-Forwarded-For"); forwarded != "" {
   214→				ip = strings.Split(forwarded, ",")[0]
   215→			}
   216→			if realIP := r.Header.Get("X-Real-IP"); realIP != "" {
   217→				ip = realIP
   218→			}
   219→
   220→			bucket := limiter.getBucket(ip, capacity, refillRate)
   221→			if !bucket.allow() {
   222→				respondError(w, http.StatusTooManyRequests, "rate limit exceeded", "rate_limit_exceeded")
   223→				return
   224→			}
   225→
   226→			next.ServeHTTP(w, r)
   227→		})
   228→	}
   229→}
   230→"}]},"uuid":"ffacbf8c-b33a-4eca-af41-20a29ade8c6b","timestamp":"2026-02-17T23:25:21.242Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"d6bd48c4-cb95-47d7-a673-ce508faaf782"}

    23→// TestServer holds test server dependencies

    29→// NewTestServer creates a new test server with in-memory SQLite database
    30→func NewTestServer(t *testing.T) *TestServer {
    31→	t.Helper()
    32→
    33→	// Use fast bcrypt for tests (MinCost=4 vs production Cost=12)
    34→	auth.SetBcryptCost(bcrypt.MinCost)
    35→
    36→	// Create test logger
    37→	logger := zaptest.NewLogger(t)
    38→
    39→	// Create in-memory database
    40→	cfg := db.Config{
    41→		DBPath:         ":memory:",
    42→		MigrationsPath: "./../../internal/db/migrations",
    43→	}
    44→
    45→	database, err := db.New(cfg, logger)
    46→	if err != nil {
    47→		t.Fatalf("Failed to create test database: %v", err)
    48→	}
    49→
    50→	// Create test config
    51→	testCfg := &config.Config{
    52→		JWTSecret:      "test-secret-key",
    53→		JWTExpiryHours: 24,
    54→	}
    55→
    56→	server := NewServer(database, testCfg, logger)
    57→
    58→	return &TestServer{
    59→		Server: server,
    60→		DB:     database,
    61→	}
    62→}

    64→// Close cleans up test server resources
    65→func (ts *TestServer) Close() {

    67→		ts.DB.Close()
    68→	}
    69→}

    71→// CreateTestUser creates a user for testing and returns the user ID
    72→func (ts *TestServer) CreateTestUser(t *testing.T, email, password string) int64 {

    74→
    75→	hashedPassword, err := auth.HashPassword(password)
    76→	if err != nil {
    77→		t.Fatalf("Failed to hash password: %v", err)
    78→	}
    79→
    80→	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)
    81→	defer cancel()
    82→
    83→	query := `INSERT INTO users (email, password_hash) VALUES (?, ?) RETURNING id`
    84→	var userID int64
    85→	err = ts.DB.QueryRowContext(ctx, query, email, hashedPassword).Scan(&userID)
    86→	if err != nil {
    87→		t.Fatalf("Failed to create test user: %v", err)
    88→	}
    89→
    90→	return userID
    91→}

   125→// MakeRequest is a helper to make HTTP requests in tests
   126→// Returns both the ResponseRecorder and the Request for testing
   127→func MakeRequest(t *testing.T, method, path string, body interface{}, headers map[string]string) (*httptest.ResponseRecorder, *http.Request) {
   128→	t.Helper()
   129→
   130→	var reqBody []byte
   131→	var err error
   132→	if body != nil {
   133→		reqBody, err = json.Marshal(body)
   134→		if err != nil {
   135→			t.Fatalf("Failed to marshal request body: %v", err)
   136→		}
   137→	}
   138→
   139→	req := httptest.NewRequest(method, path, bytes.NewBuffer(reqBody))
   140→	req.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
   141→
   142→	for key, value := range headers {
   143→		req.Header.Set(key, value)
   144→	}
   145→
   146→	return httptest.NewRecorder(), req
   147→}
   148→
   149→// DecodeJSON decodes a JSON response into the provided interface
   150→func DecodeJSON(t *testing.T, rec *httptest.ResponseRecorder, v interface{}) {
   151→	t.Helper()
   152→
   153→	if err := json.NewDecoder(rec.Body).Decode(v); err != nil {
   154→		t.Fatalf("Failed to decode JSON response: %v", err)
   155→	}

   260→// MakeAuthRequest creates an HTTP request with auth context (UserIDKey) and optional chi URL params
   261→func (ts *TestServer) MakeAuthRequest(t *testing.T, method, path string, body interface{}, userID int64, urlParams map[string]string) (*httptest.ResponseRecorder, *http.Request) {
   262→	t.Helper()
   263→
   264→	rec, req := MakeRequest(t, method, path, body, nil)
   265→
   266→	ctx := context.WithValue(req.Context(), UserIDKey, userID)
   267→
   268→	if len(urlParams) > 0 {
   269→		rctx := chi.NewRouteContext()
   270→		for k, v := range urlParams {
   271→			rctx.URLParams.Add(k, v)
   272→		}
   273→		ctx = context.WithValue(ctx, chi.RouteCtxKey, rctx)
   274→	}
   275→
   276→	req = req.WithContext(ctx)
   277→	return rec, req
   278→}

   280→// AssertError checks if the error response matches expected error and code
   281→func AssertError(t *testing.T, rec *httptest.ResponseRecorder, wantCode int, wantErrorContains, wantCodeContains string) {
   282→	t.Helper()
   283→
   284→	AssertStatusCode(t, rec.Code, wantCode)
   285→
   286→	var errResp ErrorResponse
   287→	DecodeJSON(t, rec, &errResp)
   288→
   289→	if wantErrorContains != "" && !contains(errResp.Error, wantErrorContains) {
   290→		t.Errorf("Error message %q does not contain %q", errResp.Error, wantErrorContains)
   291→	}
   292→
   293→	if wantCodeContains != "" && !contains(errResp.Code, wantCodeContains) {
   294→		t.Errorf("Error code %q does not contain %q", errResp.Code, wantCodeContains)
   295→	}
   296→}
   297→
   298→// contains checks if a string contains a substring
   299→func contains(s, substr string) bool {
   300→	return len(s) >= len(substr) && (s == substr || indexOf(s, substr) >= 0)
   301→}
   302→
   303→// indexOf returns the index of the first occurrence of substr in s, or -1
   304→func indexOf(s, substr string) int {
   305→	for i := 0; i <= len(s)-len(substr); i++ {
   306→		if s[i:i+len(substr)] == substr {
   307→			return i
   308→		}
   309→	}
   310→	return -1
   311→}

   470→func TestGetClientIP(t *testing.T) {
   471→	tests := []struct {
   472→		name   string
   473→		xff    string
   474→		xri    string
   475→		remote string
   476→		want   string
   477→	}{
   478→		{"X-Forwarded-For single", "1.2.3.4", "", "5.6.7.8:1234", "1.2.3.4"},
   479→		{"X-Forwarded-For multiple", "1.2.3.4, 10.0.0.1", "", "5.6.7.8:1234", "1.2.3.4"},
   480→		{"X-Real-IP", "", "9.8.7.6", "5.6.7.8:1234", "9.8.7.6"},
   481→		{"RemoteAddr fallback", "", "", "5.6.7.8:1234", "5.6.7.8:1234"},
   482→	}
   483→
   484→	for _, tt := range tests {
   485→		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
   486→			req, _ := http.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, "/", nil)
   487→			if tt.xff != "" {
   488→				req.Header.Set("X-Forwarded-For", tt.xff)
   489→			}
   490→			if tt.xri != "" {
   491→				req.Header.Set("X-Real-IP", tt.xri)
   492→			}
   493→			req.RemoteAddr = tt.remote
   494→
   495→			got := getClientIP(req)
   496→			if got != tt.want {
   497→				t.Errorf("getClientIP() = %q, want %q", got, tt.want)
   498→			}
   499→		})
   500→	}
   501→}

    12→func TestHandleCreateAPIKey(t *testing.T) {
    13→	tests := []struct {
    14→		name          string
    15→		keyName       string
    16→		expiresIn     *int
    17→		wantStatus    int
    18→		wantError     string
    19→		wantErrorCode string
    20→	}{
    21→		{
    22→			name:       "valid API key",
    23→			keyName:    "Test API Key",
    24→			wantStatus: http.StatusCreated,
    25→		},
    26→		{
    27→			name:          "missing name",
    28→			keyName:       "",
    29→			wantStatus:    http.StatusBadRequest,
    30→			wantError:     "name is required",
    31→			wantErrorCode: "validation_error",
    32→		},
    33→		{
    34→			name:       "with expiration",
    35→			keyName:    "Expiring Key",
    36→			expiresIn:  intPtr(90),
    37→			wantStatus: http.StatusCreated,
    38→		},
    39→	}
    40→
    41→	for _, tt := range tests {
    42→		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
    43→			ts := NewTestServer(t)
    44→			defer ts.Close()
    45→
    46→			userID := ts.CreateTestUser(t, "apikey@example.com", "password123")
    47→
    48→			req := CreateAPIKeyRequest{
    49→				Name:      tt.keyName,
    50→				ExpiresIn: tt.expiresIn,
    51→			}
    52→
    53→			rec, httpReq := MakeRequest(t, http.MethodPost, "/api/api-keys", req, nil)
    54→
    55→			// Add user to context
    56→			ctx := context.WithValue(httpReq.Context(), UserIDKey, userID)
    57→			httpReq = httpReq.WithContext(ctx)
    58→
    59→			ts.HandleCreateAPIKey(rec, httpReq)
    60→
    61→			AssertStatusCode(t, rec.Code, tt.wantStatus)
    62→
    63→			if tt.wantError != "" {
    64→				AssertError(t, rec, tt.wantStatus, tt.wantError, tt.wantErrorCode)
    65→			} else {
    66→				var resp CreateAPIKeyResponse
    67→				DecodeJSON(t, rec, &resp)
    68→
    69→				if resp.Name != tt.keyName {
    70→					t.Errorf("Expected name %q, got %q", tt.keyName, resp.Name)
    71→				}
    72→
    73→				if resp.Key == "" {
    74→					t.Error("Expected non-empty API key")
    75→				}
    76→
    77→				if len(resp.KeyPrefix) != 8 {
    78→					t.Errorf("Expected key prefix length 8, got %d", len(resp.KeyPrefix))
    79→				}
    80→			}
    81→		})
    82→	}
    83→}

   115→func TestGenerateAPIKey(t *testing.T) {

   140→func TestHandleDeleteAPIKey(t *testing.T) {
   141→	tests := []struct {
   142→		name          string
   143→		setupFunc     func(*TestServer) (userID int64, keyID string)
   144→		wantStatus    int
   145→		wantError     string
   146→		wantErrorCode string
   147→		noAuth        bool
   148→	}{
   149→		{
   150→			name: "delete own API key successfully",
   151→			setupFunc: func(ts *TestServer) (int64, string) {
   152→				userID := ts.CreateTestUser(t, "user@example.com", "password123")
   153→				apiKey, err := ts.DB.CreateAPIKey(context.Background(), userID, "My Key", nil)
   154→				if err != nil {
   155→					t.Fatalf("Failed to create API key: %v", err)
   156→				}
   157→				return userID, fmt.Sprintf("%d", apiKey.ID)
   158→			},
   159→			wantStatus: http.StatusNoContent,
   160→		},
   161→		{
   162→			name: "delete non-existent key returns not found",
   163→			setupFunc: func(ts *TestServer) (int64, string) {
   164→				userID := ts.CreateTestUser(t, "user@example.com", "password123")
   165→				return userID, "99999"
   166→			},
   167→			wantStatus:    http.StatusNotFound,
   168→			wantError:     "API key not found",
   169→			wantErrorCode: "not_found",
   170→		},
   171→		{
   172→			name: "cannot delete another user's key",
   173→			setupFunc: func(ts *TestServer) (int64, string) {
   174→				ownerID := ts.CreateTestUser(t, "owner@example.com", "password123")
   175→				apiKey, err := ts.DB.CreateAPIKey(context.Background(), ownerID, "Owner Key", nil)
   176→				if err != nil {
   177→					t.Fatalf("Failed to create API key: %v", err)
   178→				}
   179→				// Return a different user as the requester
   180→				otherUserID := ts.CreateTestUser(t, "other@example.com", "password123")
   181→				return otherUserID, fmt.Sprintf("%d", apiKey.ID)
   182→			},
   183→			wantStatus:    http.StatusNotFound,
   184→			wantError:     "API key not found",
   185→			wantErrorCode: "not_found",
   186→		},
   187→		{
   188→			name: "invalid key ID format",
   189→			setupFunc: func(ts *TestServer) (int64, string) {
   190→				userID := ts.CreateTestUser(t, "user@example.com", "password123")
   191→				return userID, "not-a-number"
   192→			},
   193→			wantStatus:    http.StatusBadRequest,
   194→			wantError:     "invalid API key ID",
   195→			wantErrorCode: "invalid_request",
   196→		},
   197→		{
   198→			name:          "unauthenticated request",
   199→			noAuth:        true,
   200→			wantStatus:    http.StatusUnauthorized,
   201→			wantError:     "unauthorized",
   202→			wantErrorCode: "unauthorized",
   203→		},
   204→	}
   205→
   206→	for _, tt := range tests {
   207→		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
   208→			ts := NewTestServer(t)
   209→			defer ts.Close()
   210→
   211→			if tt.noAuth {
   212→				rec, req := MakeRequest(t, http.MethodDelete, "/api/api-keys/1", nil, nil)
   213→				ts.HandleDeleteAPIKey(rec, req)
   214→
   215→				AssertStatusCode(t, rec.Code, tt.wantStatus)
   216→				if tt.wantError != "" {
   217→					AssertError(t, rec, tt.wantStatus, tt.wantError, tt.wantErrorCode)
   218→				}
   219→				return
   220→			}
   221→
   222→			userID, keyIDStr := tt.setupFunc(ts)
   223→
   224→			urlParams := map[string]string{"id": keyIDStr}
   225→			rec, req := ts.MakeAuthRequest(t, http.MethodDelete, "/api/api-keys/"+keyIDStr, nil, userID, urlParams)
   226→			ts.HandleDeleteAPIKey(rec, req)
   227→
   228→			AssertStatusCode(t, rec.Code, tt.wantStatus)
   229→
   230→			if tt.wantError != "" {
   231→				AssertError(t, rec, tt.wantStatus, tt.wantError, tt.wantErrorCode)
   232→			}
   233→
   234→			// For successful delete, verify the key is actually gone
   235→			if tt.wantStatus == http.StatusNoContent {
   236→				keys, err := ts.DB.GetAPIKeysByUserID(context.Background(), userID)
   237→				if err != nil {
   238→					t.Fatalf("Failed to list API keys: %v", err)
   239→				}
   240→				if len(keys) != 0 {
   241→					t.Errorf("Expected 0 API keys after delete, got %d", len(keys))
   242→				}
   243→			}
   244→		})
   245→	}
   246→}

    34→// NewTestServer creates a new test server with in-memory database
    35→func NewTestServer(t *testing.T) *TestServer {
    36→	t.Helper()
    37→
    38→	// Create temporary database
    39→	tmpDB := fmt.Sprintf(":memory:")
    40→
    41→	// Create server configuration
    42→	cfg := &config.Config{
    43→		Port:               "0", // Random port
    44→		Env:                "test",
    45→		DBPath:             tmpDB,
    46→		MigrationsPath:     "../db/migrations",
    47→		JWTSecret:          "test-secret-key-for-e2e-tests",
    48→		JWTExpiryHours:     24,
    49→		CORSAllowedOrigins: []string{"*"},
    50→		LogLevel:           "error",
    51→	}
    52→
    53→	// Initialize logger
    54→	logger := config.MustInitLogger(cfg.Env, cfg.LogLevel)
    55→
    56→	// Initialize database
    57→	dbCfg := db.Config{
    58→		DBPath:         tmpDB,
    59→		MigrationsPath: "../db/migrations",
    60→	}
    61→
    62→	database, err := db.New(dbCfg, logger)
    63→	if err != nil {
    64→		t.Fatalf("Failed to create database: %v", err)
    65→	}
    66→
    67→	// Create API server
    68→	server := api.NewServer(database, cfg, logger)
    69→
    70→	// Setup router
    71→	r := chi.NewRouter()
    72→
    73→	// Middleware stack
    74→	r.Use(middleware.RequestID)
    75→	r.Use(middleware.RealIP)
    76→	r.Use(middleware.Recoverer)
    77→	r.Use(middleware.Timeout(30 * time.Second))
    78→
    79→	// CORS configuration for tests
    80→	r.Use(cors.Handler(cors.Options{
    81→		AllowedOrigins:   []string{"*"},
    82→		AllowedMethods:   []string{"GET", "POST", "PUT", "PATCH", "DELETE", "OPTIONS"},
    83→		AllowedHeaders:   []string{"Accept", "Authorization", "Content-Type"},
    84→		ExposedHeaders:   []string{"Link"},
    85→		AllowCredentials: true,
    86→		MaxAge:           300,
    87→	}))
    88→
    89→	// Public routes
    90→	r.Get("/", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
    91→		w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
    92→		w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
    93→		fmt.Fprintf(w, `{"message":"TaskAI API","version":"0.1.0"}`)
    94→	})
    95→
    96→	r.Get("/healthz", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
    97→		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(r.Context(), 2*time.Second)
    98→		defer cancel()
    99→
   100→		if err := database.HealthCheck(ctx); err != nil {
   101→			w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
   102→			w.WriteHeader(http.StatusServiceUnavailable)
   103→			fmt.Fprintf(w, `{"status":"error","message":"database unavailable"}`)
   104→			return
   105→		}
   106→
   107→		w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
   108→		w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
   109→		fmt.Fprintf(w, `{"status":"ok","database":"connected"}`)
   110→	})
   111→
   112→	// API routes
   113→	r.Route("/api", func(r chi.Router) {
   114→		r.Get("/openapi", server.HandleOpenAPI)
   115→
   116→		// Auth routes (public)
   117→		r.Route("/auth", func(r chi.Router) {
   118→			r.Post("/signup", server.HandleSignup)
   119→			r.Post("/login", server.HandleLogin)
   120→		})
   121→
   122→		// Protected routes
   123→		r.Group(func(r chi.Router) {
   124→			r.Use(server.JWTAuth)
   125→
   126→			r.Get("/me", server.HandleMe)
   127→
   128→			// Project routes
   129→			r.Get("/projects", server.HandleListProjects)
   130→			r.Post("/projects", server.HandleCreateProject)
   131→			r.Get("/projects/{id}", server.HandleGetProject)
   132→			r.Patch("/projects/{id}", server.HandleUpdateProject)
   133→			r.Delete("/projects/{id}", server.HandleDeleteProject)
   134→
   135→			// Task routes
   136→			r.Get("/projects/{projectId}/tasks", server.HandleListTasks)
   137→			r.Post("/projects/{projectId}/tasks", server.HandleCreateTask)
   138→			r.Patch("/tasks/{id}", server.HandleUpdateTask)
   139→			r.Delete("/tasks/{id}", server.HandleDeleteTask)
   140→
   141→			// Team routes
   142→			r.Route("/team", func(r chi.Router) {
   143→				r.Get("/", server.HandleGetMyTeam)
   144→				r.Get("/members", server.HandleGetTeamMembers)
   145→				r.Post("/invitations", server.HandleInviteTeamMember)
   146→				r.Get("/invitations", server.HandleGetMyInvitations)
   147→				r.Post("/invitations/{id}/accept", server.HandleAcceptInvitation)
   148→				r.Post("/invitations/{id}/reject", server.HandleRejectInvitation)
   149→				r.Delete("/members/{memberId}", server.HandleRemoveTeamMember)
   150→			})
   151→		})
   152→	})
   153→
   154→	// Find available port
   155→	listener, err := net.Listen("tcp", ":0")
   156→	if err != nil {
   157→		t.Fatalf("Failed to find available port: %v", err)
   158→	}
   159→	port := listener.Addr().(*net.TCPAddr).Port
   160→	listener.Close()
   161→
   162→	// Create HTTP server
   163→	httpServer := &http.Server{
   164→		Addr:    fmt.Sprintf(":%d", port),
   165→		Handler: r,
   166→	}
   167→
   168→	// Start server in background
   169→	go func() {
   170→		if err := httpServer.ListenAndServe(); err != nil && err != http.ErrServerClosed {
   171→			t.Logf("Server error: %v", err)
   172→		}
   173→	}()
   174→
   175→	// Wait for server to be ready
   176→	baseURL := fmt.Sprintf("http://localhost:%d", port)
   177→	client := &http.Client{Timeout: 10 * time.Second}
   178→	
   179→	for i := 0; i < 50; i++ {
   180→		resp, err := client.Get(baseURL + "/healthz")
   181→		if err == nil {
   182→			resp.Body.Close()
   183→			if resp.StatusCode == http.StatusOK {
   184→				break
   185→			}
   186→		}
   187→		time.Sleep(10 * time.Millisecond)
   188→	}
   189→
   190→	cleanup := func() {
   191→		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)
   192→		defer cancel()
   193→		httpServer.Shutdown(ctx)
   194→		database.Close()
   195→	}
   196→
   197→	return &TestServer{
   198→		Server:  httpServer,
   199→		BaseURL: baseURL,
   200→		DB:      database.DB,
   201→		Client:  client,
   202→		cleanup: cleanup,
   203→	}
   204→}

   206→// Close cleans up the test server
   207→func (ts *TestServer) Close() {
   208→	if ts.cleanup != nil {
   209→		ts.cleanup()
   210→	}
   211→}

   213→// Helper functions for API calls
   214→
   215→// DoRequest makes an HTTP request and returns the response
   216→func (ts *TestServer) DoRequest(method, path string, body interface{}, token string) (*http.Response, error) {
   217→	var bodyReader io.Reader
   218→	if body != nil {
   219→		jsonBody, err := json.Marshal(body)
   220→		if err != nil {
   221→			return nil, err
   222→		}
   223→		bodyReader = bytes.NewBuffer(jsonBody)
   224→	}
   225→
   226→	req, err := http.NewRequest(method, ts.BaseURL+path, bodyReader)
   227→	if err != nil {
   228→		return nil, err
   229→	}
   230→
   231→	req.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
   232→	if token != "" {
   233→		req.Header.Set("Authorization", "Bearer "+token)
   234→	}
   235→
   236→	return ts.Client.Do(req)
   237→}
   238→
   239→// ParseJSON parses JSON response
   240→func ParseJSON(resp *http.Response, v interface{}) error {
   241→	defer resp.Body.Close()
   242→	return json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(v)
   243→}
   244→
   245→// Signup creates a new user
   246→func (ts *TestServer) Signup(email, password string) (string, map[string]interface{}, error) {
   247→	body := map[string]string{
   248→		"email":    email,
   249→		"password": password,
   250→	}
   251→
   252→	resp, err := ts.DoRequest("POST", "/api/auth/signup", body, "")
   253→	if err != nil {
   254→		return "", nil, err
   255→	}
   256→
   257→	if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusCreated {
   258→		return "", nil, fmt.Errorf("signup failed with status %d", resp.StatusCode)
   259→	}
   260→
   261→	var result map[string]interface{}
   262→	if err := ParseJSON(resp, &result); err != nil {
   263→		return "", nil, err
   264→	}
   265→
   266→	token, ok := result["token"].(string)
   267→	if !ok {
   268→		return "", nil, fmt.Errorf("no token in response")
   269→	}
   270→
   271→	return token, result, nil
   272→}

   757→func TestTeamProjectAccess(t *testing.T) {
   758→	ts := NewTestServer(t)
   759→	defer ts.Close()
   760→
   761→	// Create first user
   762→	user1Email := fmt.Sprintf("user1-%d@example.com", time.Now().UnixNano())
   763→	user1Token, _, err := ts.Signup(user1Email, "password123")
   764→	if err != nil {
   765→		t.Fatalf("Signup user1 failed: %v", err)
   766→	}
   767→
   768→	// Create a second user
   769→	user2Email := fmt.Sprintf("user2-%d@example.com", time.Now().UnixNano())
   770→	user2Token, _, err := ts.Signup(user2Email, "password123")
   771→	if err != nil {
   772→		t.Fatalf("Signup user2 failed: %v", err)
   773→	}
   774→
   775→	// User1 creates a project
   776→	project, err := ts.CreateProject(user1Token, "Shared Project", "Should be visible to team members")
   777→	if err != nil {
   778→		t.Fatalf("Create project failed: %v", err)
   779→	}
   780→
   781→	// User1 invites user2 to their team
   782→	inviteBody := map[string]string{"email": user2Email}
   783→	inviteResp, err := ts.DoRequest("POST", "/api/team/invitations", inviteBody, user1Token)
   784→	if err != nil {
   785→		t.Fatalf("Invite failed: %v", err)
   786→	}
   787→	if inviteResp.StatusCode != http.StatusCreated {
   788→		body, _ := io.ReadAll(inviteResp.Body)
   789→		t.Fatalf("Expected status 201, got %d: %s", inviteResp.StatusCode, string(body))
   790→	}
   791→
   792→	// User2 gets their invitations
   793→	listInvResp, err := ts.DoRequest("GET", "/api/team/invitations", nil, user2Token)
   794→	if err != nil {
   795→		t.Fatalf("List invitations failed: %v", err)
   796→	}
   797→	var invitations []map[string]interface{}
   798→	if err := json.NewDecoder(listInvResp.Body).Decode(&invitations); err != nil {
   799→		t.Fatalf("Decode invitations failed: %v", err)
   800→	}
   801→	if len(invitations) == 0 {
   802→		t.Fatalf("Expected at least one invitation, got 0")
   803→	}
   804→	invitationID := int(invitations[0]["id"].(float64))
   805→
   806→	// User2 accepts the invitation
   807→	acceptPath := fmt.Sprintf("/api/team/invitations/%d/accept", invitationID)
   808→	acceptResp, err := ts.DoRequest("POST", acceptPath, nil, user2Token)
   809→	if err != nil {
   810→		t.Fatalf("Accept invitation failed: %v", err)
   811→	}
   812→	if acceptResp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
   813→		body, _ := io.ReadAll(acceptResp.Body)
   814→		t.Fatalf("Expected status 200, got %d: %s", acceptResp.StatusCode, string(body))
   815→	}
   816→
   817→	// User2 should now be able to see the project
   818→	listProjectsResp, err := ts.DoRequest("GET", "/api/projects", nil, user2Token)
   819→	if err != nil {
   820→		t.Fatalf("List projects failed: %v", err)
   821→	}
   822→	var projects []map[string]interface{}
   823→	if err := json.NewDecoder(listProjectsResp.Body).Decode(&projects); err != nil {
   824→		t.Fatalf("Decode projects failed: %v", err)
   825→	}
   826→
   827→	// Verify user2 can see the project
   828→	found := false
   829→	projectID := int64(project["id"].(float64))
   830→	for _, p := range projects {
   831→		if int64(p["id"].(float64)) == projectID {
   832→			found = true
   833→			if p["name"].(string) != "Shared Project" {
   834→				t.Errorf("Expected project name 'Shared Project', got %s", p["name"].(string))
   835→			}
   836→			break
   837→		}
   838→	}
   839→	if !found {
   840→		t.Errorf("User2 cannot see the shared project (expected to find project ID %d)", projectID)
   841→	}
   842→
   843→	// User1 creates a new project after user2 joined
   844→	newProject, err := ts.CreateProject(user1Token, "New Project After Join", "Created after user2 joined team")
   845→	if err != nil {
   846→		t.Fatalf("Create new project failed: %v", err)
   847→	}
   848→
   849→	// User2 should also see this new project
   850→	listProjectsResp2, err := ts.DoRequest("GET", "/api/projects", nil, user2Token)
   851→	if err != nil {
   852→		t.Fatalf("List projects failed: %v", err)
   853→	}
   854→	var projects2 []map[string]interface{}
   855→	if err := json.NewDecoder(listProjectsResp2.Body).Decode(&projects2); err != nil {
   856→		t.Fatalf("Decode projects failed: %v", err)
   857→	}
   858→
   859→	found2 := false
   860→	newProjectID := int64(newProject["id"].(float64))
   861→	for _, p := range projects2 {
   862→		if int64(p["id"].(float64)) == newProjectID {
   863→			found2 = true
   864→			break
   865→		}
   866→	}
   867→	if !found2 {
   868→		t.Errorf("User2 cannot see the new project created after they joined (expected to find project ID %d)", newProjectID)
   869→	}
   870→}
